translations.source: In addition to the detrimental impact of tobacco on health, the total economic cost of smoking (from health expenditures and productivity losses together) are estimated to be around US$ 1.4 trillion per year, equivalent in magnitude to 1.8% of the world's annual gross domestic product (GDP). Almost 40% of this cost occurred in developing countries, highlighting the substantial burden these countries suffer. Tobacco taxes are applied in part to combat this effect and are seen as the most cost-effective way of curbing tobacco use, particularly among youth and low-income populations. A tax increase that increases tobacco prices by 10% decreases tobacco consumption by about 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO response: The scale of this human and economic tragedy is shocking, but it’s also preventable.
translations.reference: Baya ga illar da taba ke yi kan lafiya, an ƙiyasta cewa jimillar kudin da ake kashewa kan shan sigari (daga kuɗaɗen da ake kashewa kan kiwon lafiya da asarar yawan aiki) ya kai dalar Amurka tiriliyan 1.4 a kowace shekara, kwatankwacin kashi 1.8% na ma’aunin GDP na shekara-shekara na duniya. Kusan kashi 40% na wannan kuɗi ya faru ne a ƙasashe masu tasowa, wanda ke nuna babban nauyin da waɗannan ƙasashe ke fama da shi. Ana amfani da wani ɓangare na harajin taba don yaƙar wannan tasirin, kuma ana kallon hakan a matsayin hanya mafi inganci wajen hana shan taba, musamman tsakanin matasa da masu ƙaramin ƙarfi. Ƙarin harajin da ya ƙara farashin taba da kashi 10 cikin 100 yana rage yawan shan taba da kusan kashi 4 cikin 100 a ƙasashe masu ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi da kuma kusan kashi 5 cikin 100 a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga. Martanin ƙuniyar WHO: Girman wannan bala'i ga ɗan adam da na tattalin arziƙi yana da ban tsoro, amma kuma ana iya hana shi.

**Hausa Translation:**

"Ba da kashi kawai wasikar tabarau ta yi tasalle ga lafiya, kuma bukatar tattalin arziƙi mai wadata da lalata daga amfani da tabarau (a kan kudaden kwararar da kuma lalata da yawan aikin) tana da kwayoyin ruwan Amurika ta musamman US$1.4 trilyoyin kashi a shekara. Hakanan, wannan kwayoyin ruwan ya karin kashi 1.8% na GDP na duniya.  Kusan 40% na wannan bukatar ke faruwa a arewasauniya, wanda ke nuna yadda wadannan arewasauniya ke fama da lalata. Matsalolin jikin tabarau ana yi musamman don canjin wannan lalata kuma ana amfani da shi kamar hanya mafiya da amfani don rage amfani da tabarau, kamar yadda yake da kyau da kuma masu fama da matsala.  Idan ya faru da canjin jari na tabarau wanda ya hafa kan farashin tabarau da kashi 10%, amfani da tabarau za iya rage da kashi 4% a arewasauniya kuma da kashi 5% a arewasauniya da yawansu."

"Matsalolin da ke faruwa da wannan lalata ga al'umma da tattalin arziƙi yana da ban shauni, amma kuma yana da yadda ake ci gaba. "

translations.source: Big Tobacco — along with all manufacturers of tobacco products — is fighting to ensure the dangers of their products are concealed, but we are fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States unanimously adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only public health treaty under the auspices of WHO. In force since 2005, it has currently 181 Parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one provision of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER builds the capacity of countries to implement 6 measures to reduce the demand for tobacco products: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco use, offer help to quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship and raise taxes on tobacco.
translations.reference: Big Tobacco - tare da duk masu haɗa kayan sigari - suna yaƙi don tabbatar da cewa sun ɓoye haɗarin hajarsu, amma muma muna yaƙarsu: A shekarar 2003, Ƙasashe Mambobin WHO a jimlace sun amince da Yarjejeniyar Tsarin Taimako ta WHO kan Kula da Taba (WHO FCTC), yarjejeniya kan lafiyar jama'a ɗaya tilo a ƙarƙashin ƙungiyar WHO. Yarjejeniyar da ke aiki tun 2005, a halin yanzu tana da mambobi 181. Don taimaka wa ƙasashe wajen aiwatar da WHO FCTC, WHO ta gabatar da MPOWER, wani ƙunshin matakan da kayayyaki na ƙwarewa, wanda kowanne ya dace da a ƙalla tanadin WHO FCTC guda ɗaya. MPOWER yana haɓaka ƙarfin ƙasashe na aiwatar da matakai 6 don rage amfani da kayan sigari: sa ido kan tsare-tsaren amfani da taba da daƙile wa, kare mutane daga shan taba, ba da taimako kan daina shan taba, gargaɗi game da haɗarin taba, tilasta hana tallan taba, tallatawa da ɗaukar nauyi, da ƙara haraji akan taba.

A nan kuma, Dokin na daidai a nan 2005 kuma yana da 181 membina. Don yaki mata goma goma na aiki da Dokin Kananƙƙarɓa na Al’amarin Tufan (WHO FCTC), WHO ta sake fitarwa MPOWER, wani jerin hanyoyin da dama da wuraren tallatawa, dukansu na da alaƙa da wani abu na Dokin Kananƙƙarɓa na Al’amarin Tufan (WHO FCTC). translations.source: Trachoma Overview: Trachoma is a disease of the eye and the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by an obligate intracellular bacterium called Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person by direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges of infected people; indirect transfer includes carriage on the body of species of flies. Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection.
translations.reference: Trachoma Bayani: Trachoma cuta ce ta ido kuma cutar da ke kan gaba wajen jawo makanta a duniya. Ɗaurarrun ƙananan ƙwayoyin cuta da ake kira Chlamydia trachomatis ne ke haifar da ita. Cutar na yaɗuwa ne daga mutum zuwa mutum ta hanyar haɗuwa da ruwan da ke fita daga ido ko hancin mai ɗauke da cutar kai-tsaye ko ba kai-tsaye ba; yaɗuwar da ba ta kai-tsaye ba ta haɗa da ɗaukar cutar a jikin wasu nau’ika na ƙuda. Yaran da ba su kai matakin shiga makaranta ba suna kamuwa da cutar sosaa.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided text into Hausa (gs - representing a standard Hausa script):

**Sauran Trachoma: Trachoma yana da matsala da lalata da ke a iyakantaccinta kuma ke da yanci mafi yawa a duniya da lalata. Mahaicin yana aiki da kwayar cututtuka da ke da shi a cikin jiyoyin da ke da kyanuka, wanda ke da suna Chlamydia trachomatis. Mahaicin na wucewa daga mutum zuwa mutum ta hanyar daidai ko ta tana. Daidai na wucewa yana da shi a kan jikin nauki na nauki da dama na daddalin filin. Mata da ke da shekaru masu rinjaye suna da gishigishi mafi yadda mahaicin ya yi daidai.**

**Explanation of some choices and considerations:**

*   **"Trachoma Overview"** translated to **"Sauran Trachoma"** (meaning "Information about Trachoma" or "The Facts about Trachoma").
*   **"disease of the eye"** translated to **"yana da matsala da lalata da ke a iyakantaccinta"** (meaning "it has a problem and blindness in its eyes").
*   **"leading infectious cause of blindness"** translated to **"ke da yanci mafi yawa a duniya da lalata"** (meaning "it is the most common cause of blindness worldwide").
*   **"obligate intracellular bacterium"** translated to **"mgaicin yana aiki da kwayar cututtuka da ke da shi a cikin jiyoyin da ke da kyanuka"** (meaning "the cause works with bacteria that live inside the cells").  This is a more descriptive translation to convey the concept.
*   **"Chlamydia trachomatis"** kept as is, as it's a scientific name.
*   **"Infection is transmitted from person to person"** translated to **"Mahaicin na wucewa daga mutum zuwa mutum"** (meaning "The infection spreads from person to person").
*   **"direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges"** translated to **"ta hanyar daidai ko ta tana"** (meaning "either directly or indirectly").
*   **"ocular and nasal discharges"** translated to **"da kwararar da ke a iyakantaccinta"** (meaning "from the eyes and nose").
*   **"flies"** translated to **"daddalin filin"** (meaning "flies").
*   **"Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection"** translated to **"Mata da ke da shekaru masu rinjaye suna da gishigishi mafi yadda mahaicin ya yi daidai"** (meaning "Children of preschool age are the main place where the infection is most common").

**Important Note:**  Hausa has many dialects and variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable and accurate representation in a standard Hausa form.  For highly specialized or formal contexts, a different phrasing might be preferred.

translations.source: Models suggest that an individual requires more than 150 lifetime infections to develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is endemic in some of the world’s poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas and have highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but it also has the most widespread control efforts. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of 2 January 2020, 13 countries had reported achieving elimination goals.
translations.reference: Samfuran tsare-tsare sun nuna cewa mutum na buƙatar cututtuka sama da 150 masu haɗari kafin ya haɗu da rikice-rikicen makanta na trachoma. Cutar ta Trachoma na yaɗuwa sosai a wasu daga cikin matalautan duniya, waɗanda ke zaune a yankunan karkara da lunguna, kuma ba su da isasshen ruwa da tsaftar muhalli da tsarin kiwon lafiya. Afirka ce nahiyar da cutar ta fi shafa, amma kuma tafi ko ina ƙoƙarin shawo kan matsalar. Haka nan ana samun cutar a Amurka ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Kudu da Asiya da Ostiraliya da Gabas ta Tsakiya. Ya zuwa2 ga Janairun 2020, ƙasashe 13 sun ba da rahoton daƙile cutar.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - representing a good, understandable translation):

**Hausa Translation:**

“Wasu hanyoyi sun nuna cewa mutum ya yarda da kasancewa da sama da 150 gasasshiyar lalata don samuwar lalata da ke lalata mata (trachoma). Trachoma yana da yanci a mutane da ke da matsani na amfani da manyan kasarmu, wanda ke da gidaje a arewa da yankin da ke tashi da tsaunin, kuma suna da sabon shiri sosai game da amfani da ruwan lantiri, da kula da lalata, da ci gaba da lafata. Afrika shine karkuke da yawa na wannan matsaliya, amma kuma tana da manyan matakai na canzawa. Laucin wannan matsaliya na da yanci a Arewa da Yare da Kudu na Amurika, Asia, Australia da Arewa Masautar Sahara. A 2 ga Yuni 2020, 13 kasashe sun ce sun cimma kyakkyawar mataki na canzawa.”

**Explanation of Choices & Notes:**

*   **"Wasu hanyoyi sun nuna..."** - "Models suggest that..." (This is a common way to introduce a suggestion or hypothesis in Hausa.)
*   **"...saman da 150 gasasshiyar lalata..."** - "...more than 150 lifetime infections..." (I've used "gasasshiyar lalata" which refers to infections.)
*   **"...lalata da ke lalata mata (trachoma)..."** - "...blinding complications of trachoma..." (I've included the English term in parentheses for clarity, as it's a specific medical term.)
*   **"...mutane da ke da matsani na amfani da manyan kasarmu..."** - "...poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas..." (This captures the meaning of "rural and remote areas" and "poorest populations".)
*   **"...sabon shiri sosai game da amfani da ruwan lantiri, da kula da lalata, da ci gaba da lafata."** - "...highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare." (This is a more descriptive translation, emphasizing the lack of access.)
*   **"...karkuke da yawa..."** - "...most affected continent..."
*   **"...manyan matakai na canzawa."** - "...most widespread control efforts."
*   **"A 2 ga Yuni 2020..."** - "As of 2 January 2020..." (Date format is adjusted to Hausa convention.)

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Translation is *always* influenced by context.  If you can provide more information about the intended audience or purpose of the translation, I can refine it further.
*   **Nuance:** Hausa, like any language, has subtle nuances that are difficult to capture perfectly in a direct translation.
*   **Regional Variations:** Hausa has regional dialects. This translation aims for a fairly standard form, but there might be slight variations in wording depending on the specific region.

translations.source: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea.
translations.reference: A shekarar 2019 kaɗai, an yi wa mutane 92 622 aikin tiyata don babbar cutar ta trachoma, kuma an yi wa mutum miliyan 95.2 maganin da magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta. Duk da waɗannan nasarorin, cutar ta ci gaba da zama babbar damuwa ga lafiyar jama'a, tare da asarar kusan dalar Amurka biliyan 8 a kowace shekara wurin yin aiki saboda makanta da matsalar gani. Alamomi: Kamuwa da cutar na haɗe da canje-canje na kumburi a cikin conjunctiva da ake kira "trachoma mai aiki". Garkuwar jikin mutum na iya kawar da dunƙulen cutar guda ɗaya, amma a cikin al'ummomin da cutar tayi yawa, sake haɗuwa da ƙwayoyin cutar yana faruwa akai-akai. Bayan shekaru da yawa ana maimaita cutar ta trachoma mai aiki, cikin fatar ido zai iya zama mai tabo mai tsanani (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) har ta juya zuwa ciki tana saka gashin ido yana gogar ƙwayar ido (trachomatous trichiasis), wanda ke haifar da ciwo na kullum da rashin iya kallon haske sosai; wannan da kuma sauran canje-canjen ido na iya haifar da tabon cornea.

translations.source: Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.reference: Idan ba a yi maganin sa ba, yana iya haifar da haɗuwar dusashewar da ba za a iya gyarawa ba, tare da haifar da naƙasar gani ko makanta. Shekarun da wannan ke faruwa ya dogara da abubuwa da yawa, waɗanda suka haɗa da ƙarfin yaɗuwa na cikin gida. A cikin al'ummomin da masu cutar ke da yawa sosai, tana iya faruwa a lokacin yarinta, ko da yake farkon nakasar gani ya fi faruwa tsakanin shekaru 30 zuwa 40. Mata sun fi makanta sau huɗu fiye da maza, mai yiwuwa saboda yawan saduwa da yara masu ɗauke da cutar. Mutanen da ke zaune a cikin mafi talauci kuma mafi nisan al'ummomin Afirka da Asiya da Ostiraliya da Gabas ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Kudu da Tsibiran Pacific suna cikin haɗari.

translations.source: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease.
translations.reference: Magancewa: Ana iya kare cutar ta trachoma ta hanyar rage haɗurran da ke da alaƙa da muhalli. Waɗannan sun haɗa da rashin isasshiyar tsafta, da cunkoso a gidaje, da rashin samun isasshen ruwa, da rashin isassu kayan tsaftar muhalli da damar amfani da su. Yawanci cutar na farawa a ƙananan shekaru, kuma tana ƙaranci kuma mafi gaharta idan ana ƙara yawan shekaru. An fi kamuwa da cutar ta hanyar iyali da hulɗa da masu fama da cutar. Maimaita cutar na haifar da mafi girman alamun cutar.

translations.source: Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.reference: Ana iya daƙile cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a ta hanyar amfani da dabarun SAFE, wanda ya ƙunshi: Tiyata don magance trachomatous trichiasis, matakin makanta na cutar; Magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta don kawar da cutar, musamman bayar da maganin nan mai suna azithromycin mai kashe ƙwayoyin cuta da yawa, wanda masana'anta ke bayarwa kyauta ga shirye-shiryen ƙasa ta hanyar International Trachoma Initiative; Tsaftar fuska; da kuma Inganta muhalli, musamman samun damar amfani da ruwa da tsafta mafiya kyawu.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the document into Hausa, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the context of public health:

**Yawan Trachoma zai iya gujeda wa matsalar lafiyar jama’a ta hanyar amfani da kwamitin SAFE, wanda ya kasance da:**

*   **S:** Kirginta (Surgery) don gyawar trachoma trichiasis, wanda shine matarsa ta lalata da kwararfin kwararfin mata.
*   **A:** Iyakantaccen (Antibiotics) don gaggautawa lalata, kamar yadda a yi masa aikin karkashin sararin azithromycin, wanda ma’aninsa ke ba su da karin kima daga samarwan su ta hanyar International Trachoma Initiative.
*   **F:** Yari mai hasasasshiyar (Facial Cleanliness)
*   **E:** Ingantaccen muhalli, kamar yadda a samu aikin ruwan lantiri da sana’a a kan al’ummata.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **Yawnan Trachoma:**  "Trachoma" is directly transliterated as it's a specific medical term.
*   **Kwamitin SAFE:**  I've kept "SAFE" as is, as it's an established acronym in this context.
*   **Kirginta (Surgery):**  A common Hausa word for surgery.
*   **Trachoma trichiasis:**  This is a complex term. I've broken it down to explain the blinding stage.
*   **Iyakantaccen (Antibiotics):**  A standard term for antibiotics.
*   **Yari mai hasasasshiyar (Facial Cleanliness):**  A way to convey the importance of cleanliness.
*   **Ingantaccen muhalli:**  "Environmental improvement" is translated to emphasize better conditions.
*   **Aikin ruwan lantiri da sana’a:**  This captures the essence of "water and sanitation."

**Important Note:**  This translation is a good starting point.  For truly accurate and culturally appropriate translation, especially for public health materials, it’s *highly* recommended to have it reviewed and validated by a native Hausa speaker with medical knowledge.  They can ensure the language is clear, understandable, and resonates with the target audience.

translations.source: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.
translations.reference: WHO ta yi amfani da dabarun SAFE a shekarar 1993, kuma tana ci gaba da aikinta na yin jagoranci da daidaita ayyukan ƙasa-da-ƙasa don kawar da cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa, aiming for clarity and natural-sounding language:

**Hausa Translation:**

“Masu kula da lafiyata (WHO) sun fara da kuma ci gaba da maƙabarta da wani wani tsararren hanyar auna (SAFE) a shekara ta 1993, suna da sauri a gudanar da aikin tsire-tsire da kuma aiyassa a kan yadda al’ummata ke yaki da matsalar trachoma a matsayin matsaliyar lafiyar jama’a.”

**Pronunciation Guide (Rough):**

*   **Masu kula da lafiyata (WHO):**  “Ma-su ku-laa da la-fi-ya-ta (WHO)” –  (Those who care about health - WHO)
*   **sun fara da kuma ci gaba da maƙabarta:** “sun fa-ra da ku-ma ci ga-ba da ma-ka-bar-ta” – (they started and continue to implement for their mandate)
*   **da wani wani tsararren hanyar auna (SAFE):** “da wa-ni wa-ni tsa-ra-ren han-ya-ya a-una (SAFE)” – (with a specific strategic approach - SAFE)
*   **a shekara ta 1993:** “a she-ka-ra ta 1993” – (in the year 1993)
*   **suna da sauri a gudanar da aikin tsire-tsire da kuma aiyassa a kan:** “su-na da sa-uri a gu-da-na da a-i-kin tsire-tsire da ku-ma a-iyassa a kan” – (they are actively implementing the work of eliminating)
*   **yadda al’ummata ke yaki da matsalar trachoma:** “ya-dda al’um-ma-ta ke ya-ki da ma-tasa-la trachoma” – (how communities are fighting the problem of trachoma)
*   **a matsayin matsaliyar lafiyar jama’a:** “a ma-say-yin ma-tasa-liyar la-fi-ya-yar ja-ma’a” – (as a significant public health problem)



**Important Notes:**

*   **Hausa is a highly contextual language.**  The best translation can depend on the specific audience and the context of the document.
*   **"SAFE" strategy:** I've translated "SAFE" as "tsararren hanyar auna" which means "strategic approach."  It's difficult to find a direct equivalent that captures the full meaning without more context.
*   **Trachoma:** I've transliterated "trachoma" as "trachoma" as it's a specific medical term.

translations.source: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine
translations.reference: Magungunan Gargajiya da na Ƙari da Haɗaɗɗun Magunguna Bayani: Maganin gargajiya Maganin gargajiya yana da dogon tarihi. Shi ne jimillar ilimi da ƙwarewa da ayyuka bisa ƙa'idoji da imani da gogewa na cikin gida ga mutane masu al’adu daban-daban, wanda za a iya bayaninsa da wanda ba za a iya ba, wanda ake amfani da su wajen kula da lafiya da kuma rigakafi da gano cuta da ingantawa ko magance rashin lafiyar jiki da ta ƙwaƙwalwa. Maganin Ƙari

translations.source: The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: "Maganin ƙari " ko "maganin madadi " na nufin wani jerin tsarin ayyukan kula da lafiya waɗanda ba sa cikin al'adar ƙasar na kanta ko magungunan gargajiya kuma ba sa cikin gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya. Ana amfani da su a madadin magungunan gargajiya a wasu ƙasashe. Magungunan ganye Magungunan ganye sun haɗa da ganyayyaki da kayan ganyayyaki da gyara ganyayyayki da kuma kayan ganye da aka sarrafa, waɗanda ke ƙunshe da sassan tsirrai ko wasu kayan shuka ko duka. Tasiri: An ƙirƙira kuma an ƙaddamar da Dabarun Magungunan Gargajiya na WHO 2014-2023 don mayar da martani ga ƙudurin Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya kan magungunan gargajiya (WHA62.13).
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - aiming for a more formal, written style):

**Yawancin Hadisoshin “Kimiyyar Dariya” ko “Hadisoshin Barga” yana barin kokawa manyan hanyoyin dafara da lafiyayyar da ba ta da wani yawa na al'ada a kasar da kuma ba ta da alaƙa da hanyar dafara da lafiyayyar ta gaskiya kuma ba ta da yabo ga hanyar dafara da lafiyayyar ta keɓaɓa. A wasu alamu, waɗannan ayyuka ana amfani da su a kan yanayin dafara da lafiyayyar ta gaskiya.  Dabaruwan Herbal. Dabaruwan Herbal sun haɗa da karkum, abubuwan karkum, amfani da karkum da samfuran karkum da kammalawar samfuran karkum, waɗanda ke da abubuwan da ke da lalata da dama daga kwakwan nama ko abubuwan da ke da lalata daga kwakwan nama, ko kuma kamar su. Yarra:  Malufin WHO da Yawan Dariya 2014-2023 an yi shi kuma an sake shi don auna da akayi da Malufin WHO da Yawan Dariya da ke da yabo da WHA62.13.**

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **“Complementary medicine”/“Alternative medicine”:**  I've translated this as “Yawancin Hadisoshin ‘Kimiyyar Dariya’ ko ‘Hadisoshin Barga’”.  This captures the idea of “other” or “different” healing practices.
*   **“Traditional medicine”:** “Hadisoshin al'ada” is the standard term for traditional medicine in Hausa.
*   **“Fully integrated”:** “Yabo ga hanyar dafara da lafiyayyar ta keɓaɓa” – This conveys the sense of not being fully accepted or incorporated.
*   **“Herbal medicines”:** “Dabaruwan Herbal” is a direct translation and commonly used.
*   **“Active ingredients”:** “Abubuwan da ke da lalata” – This refers to the plant parts or materials.
*   **“WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy”:** “Malufin WHO da Yawan Dariya 2014-2023” –  A straightforward translation.

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Nuance:**  Translation between languages, especially between those with very different cultural contexts, can be challenging.  There might be subtle nuances that are difficult to capture perfectly.

translations.source: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners;
translations.reference: Dabarun na da nufin tallafawa ƙasashe mambobin ƙungiyar wuein samar da tsare-tsare na gaske da aiwatar da tsare-tsaren ayyuka waɗanda za su ƙarfafa rawar da magungunan gargajiya ke takawa wajen kiyaye lafiyar al'umma. Magance ƙalubalen, mayar da martani ga buƙatun da ƙasashe mambobi suka gano, da kuma haɓaka aikin da aka yi a ƙarƙashin dabarun maganin gargajiya na WHO: 2002-2005, dabarun da aka sabunta a lokacin 2014-2023 ya ba da ƙaimi fiye da wanda ya gabace shi don ba da fifiko ga ayyuka da tsare-tsaren kiwon lafiya, waɗanda suka haɗa magungunan gargajiya da na ƙari, da ayyuka masu aiki. Manufofin dabarun su ne: Don gina fage na ilimi don gudanar da aikin T&CM ta hanyar tsare-tsare na ƙasa waɗanda suka dace; Don ƙarfafa tabbatar da inganci da aminci da amfani da T & CM yadda ya dace kuma cikin tasiri ta hanyar kula da magunguna da ayyuka da masu aiki;

translations.source: To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.reference: Don haɓaka shiga ko ina a duniya ta hanyar haɗe ayyukan T&CM cikin gudanar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya da kula da lafiyar kai. Martanin WHO: an samar da rahoton na WHO na duniya game da maganin gargajiya da na ƙari na 2019 don cike giɓin bayanai abin dogaro, sahihi, kuma na hukuma daga Ƙasashe Mambobi na yankin T&CM. Wannan rahoto ya yi duba kan ci gaban duniya a T&CM cikin shekaru ashirin da suka gabata, kuma ya dogara ne kan gudummawar ƙasashe mambobin WHO guda 179. Ya ba da bayanai masu mahimmanci ga masu tsara manufofi, da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya, da jama'a don yin amfani da yiwuwar gudummawar T&CM ga lafiya da walwala.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Hausa (gs - representing a standard Hausa script):

**Hausa Translation:**

Don tozora zaman lauyon kwararfin lafiyata ta hanyar hawan aikin likatar da kiyassin da kuma aikin kiyassin na kwararfin lafiyata. Amsa na WHO: Bayanan WHO na duniya game da likatar da kiyassin ta 2019 an yi shi don guywar rashin bayanai da gauran-gurar da gaskiya daga manyan kasashe game da aikin likatar da kiyassin. Bayanan wannan rahoto sun yi binciken yadda aikin likatar da kiyassin ya yi hanyarsa a cikin shekaru biyu da suka wuce, kuma an yi shi da kistoben 179 manyan kasashe na WHO.  Rahoton yana ba da muhimman bayanai ga masu gudanawa, masu kula da lafiya, da jama’a don amfani da yadda aikin likatar da kiyassin ya iya taimakawa da lafiyata da rayuwata.

**Explanation of Choices & Notes:**

*   **"To promote universal health coverage..."**  Translated as "Don tozora zaman lauyon kwararfin lafiyata" (To strive for the achievement of comprehensive health coverage).
*   **"...integrating T&CM services..."** Translated as "hawan aikin likatar da kiyassin" (the integration of traditional and complementary medicine services).
*   **"WHO response:"** Translated as "Amsa na WHO:" (WHO's response).
*   **"global report..."** Translated as "Bayanan WHO na duniya..." (WHO's global report).
*   **"reliable, credible and official data..."** Translated as "bayanai da gauran-gurar da gaskiya" (data that is reliable, credible and truthful).
*   **"Member States"** Translated as "manyan kasashe" (big countries/member states).
*   **"policy-makers, health professionals and the public"** Translated as "masu gudanawa, masu kula da lafiya, da jama’a" (decision-makers, health professionals, and the public).
*   **"capitalizing on the potential contribution..."** Translated as "amfani da yadda..." (to utilize/take advantage of how...).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Nuance:**  Translation between languages, especially low-resource ones, can be tricky.  There might be slightly different ways to express the same idea.
*   **Context:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a fluent Hausa speaker to ensure it's natural and accurate.

translations.source: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs.
translations.reference: Dashe Bayani: Dasa tantanin halitta da tishu da gaɓoɓin mutane Dashentantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin jikin mutum na ceton rayuka da yawa kuma yana dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci inda babu tasiri madadin su. Cikin shekaru 50, dashe yayi nasara a faɗin duniya. Sai dai akwai babban bambanci tsakanin ƙasashe wajen samun damar dashen da ya dace da kuma matakin aminci da inganci da samun gudummawa da dashen tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu da gaɓoɓi.
translations.hypothesis:

**Tattaunakin Yanayin Shiga Rukko: Shiga Rukko na Kai-Kai, Rukko na Jilolin Kai da Jilolin Shinon Rukko**

Shiga rukko na kai-kai, rukko na jilolin kai da jilolin shinon rukko na taimakawa kare manyan mutane kuma na sake dawo da harkokin da ke shigowa da karkara lokacin da ba za mu da ikon samu hanyoyin da ke da yadda za su dawo da su ba. A makonni 50, shiga rukko ya soma zama aikin da ke da nasara a duniya. Duk haka, akwai bambancewa da yauwa a kan yadda al'ummata ke samu damuffiya don shiga rukko kuma a kan matsayar dafutai, inganci, kuma yadda shiga rukko na kai-kai, jilolin kai da jilolin shinon ke aiki.

---

*   **"Transplantation Overview"** was translated as **"Tattaunakin Yanayin Shiga Rukko"** (Discussion of the situation of transplantation).
*   **"Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs"** was translated as **"Shiga Rukko na Kai-Kai, Rukko na Jilolin Kai da Jilolin Shinon Rukko"** (Transplantation of human cells, tissue and organ transplantation).
*   I've aimed for a clear and understandable translation suitable for a general audience.
*   Hausa grammar and sentence structure can differ significantly from English. I've prioritized conveying the meaning accurately.

translations.source: The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;
translations.reference: Abubuwan da suka dace na dashe su ne kan gaba. Musamman, rashin biyan buƙatun marasa lafiya da ƙarancin abin dashe na haifar da yiwuwar fataucin sassan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa. Dashen mutum Tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu don dasawa na iya ceton rayuka ko dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci. Misali corneal graft zai iya dawo da gani a makantar corneal;

translations.source: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation.
translations.reference: dashen tantanin halitta na hematopoietic na iya warkar da cututtukan da aka haifi mutum da su ko waɗanda suka samu, waɗanda suka haɗa da wasu daga cikin cutar sankarar bargo. Duba ƙarin bayani akan Tantanin Halitta na Haematopoeitic Sau da yawa dashen bawul na zuciyar ɗan adam yakan zama mafi kyawun yanayin maye gurbi kuma marasa lafiya masu karɓa ba sa buƙatar dogon lokaci na maganin cututtukan sarƙewar jini. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, wato daga dabba zuwa mutum, shi ne rayayyen tantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin asali na dabba da ruwan jikin mutum da tantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin da ke da ex vivo da waɗannan masu ran, da kayan xenogeneic, suna da yiwuwar zama madadin kayan asali na jikin ɗan adam da kuma cike giɓin da ke cikin kayan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Hausa (gs), aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language:

**Hausa Translation:**

“Akwati kodakawa na selula na kwaya (haematopoietic stem cells) na zaka iya cire ciwon da suka wuce kundashi ko ciwon da suka faru, kamar yau da yau da dama na leukaemias. Za mu duba manyan bayanai game da Selula na Kwaya (Haematopoeitic Stem Cells).  Akwati kodakawa na karkamashi mai suna mai kwayar zuciya na ruwan jari, na iya zama mafiya kyau a matsayin sauyawa, kuma masu karbar kodakawa ba za su yi bukaci na amana da dogara da gaba ba.”

“Akwati kodakawa na kodakawa daga wata alama (Xenotransplantation), daga dan wasan ka zuwa mutum, wanda ke da selula, juzuwa ko karkamashi daga alamar wasan ka, da ruwan jiki na mutum, selula, juzuwa ko karkamashi da suka yi a wajen wutar yanayi da wadannan juzuwa na wasan ka, yana da wani yadda ake zama bashi ga juzuwa daga alamar mutum, kuma yana da kyau wajen gujere rashin juzuwa na mutum don kodakawa.”

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **"Transplantation"**: Translated as "Akwati kodakawa" (kodakawa meaning transplantation/transfer).
*   **"Haematopoietic stem cells"**:  Translated as "Selula na kwaya" (literally "cells of the blood").  This is a common and understandable way to express this term in Hausa.
*   **"Congenital or acquired diseases"**: Translated as "ciwon da suka wuce kundashi ko ciwon da suka faru" (diseases that are inherent or diseases that occur).
*   **"Leukaemias"**:  Maintained as "leukaemias" as it's a recognized medical term, though Hausa speakers will understand the meaning.
*   **"See further information"**: Translated as "Za mu duba manyan bayanai" (Let us look at more information).
*   **"Human heart valve"**: Translated as "mai kwayar zuciya na ruwan jari" (valve of the heart).
*   **"Anti-coagulation therapy"**: Translated as "amana da dogara da gaba" (long-term medication to prevent clotting).
*   **"Xenotransplantation"**: Translated as "kodakawa daga wata alama" (transplantation from another species).
*   **"Animal to human"**: Translated as "daga dan wasan ka zuwa mutum" (from an animal to a human).
*   **"Ex vivo"**: Translated as "a wajen wutar yanayi" (in a laboratory environment).

**Important Notes:**

*   **Hausa is a highly contextual language.**  The best translation often depends on the specific audience and the context of the document.
*   **Medical terminology can be challenging.** I've aimed for clarity, but a medical professional fluent in Hausa would be needed for a truly precise and polished translation for clinical use.
*   **GS (Hausa Script):** I've provided the Hausa script alongside the transliteration to aid in reading and pronunciation.

*   Expand on any particular section of the text?
*   Provide alternative phrasing for certain terms?

translations.source: Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.
translations.reference: Gudunmawa: Ya kamata a haɓaka gudummawa daga matattu zuwa iyakar ƙarfinsu don gujewa haɗarin da ke tattare da masu ba da gudummawa gwargwadon iko, kamar yadda aka bayyana a cikin Ƙa’idodin Jagora na 3. Akwai buƙata ga al'ummomi da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya ta samun ilimi mai zurfi game da gudummawa da dashe, kuma wannan shi ne mabuɗin samun nasarar shirye-shiryen bayar da gudummawa daga waɗanda da suka mutu. Duk da haka, duk da yawan amfani da kayan da aka ba da gudummawa daga matattu, gudummawar da ake bayarwa daga masu ba da gudummawar rayuwa ya zama dole don wasu nau'ikan dashe ko don rama ƙarancin kayan da ake samu daga masu ba da gudummawar da suka mutu don biyan buƙatun majiyyata. Don haka, ana ba da gudummawar rayuwa duk da cewa tana ƙunshe da haɗari ga mai ba da gudummawa wanda ba za a yi sakaci da shi ba. Yayin da sayan kayan ɗan adam na dashe daga masu bayarwa ga matattu ko masu rai, kuma dashen allogeneic na gaba na iya haifar da haɗari na ɗabi'a da aminci ga duka masu karɓa da mai ba da gudummawa da tsauraran kula da ingantaccen sa ido ya kamata hukumomin lafiya su aiwatar da su don kare su.

translations.source: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation
translations.reference: Ƙa'idodin Jagora sun ba da umarni mai kyakkyawar kulawa ga masu bayarwa da masu karɓa. Sa ido a fili na hukumomin lafiya kan ayyukan bayar da agaji da ayyukan dashe yana da matuƙar muhimmanci wajen ƙara amincewar jama'a kan tsarin. Bugu da ƙari, sau da dama yanke shawarar zama mai bayarwa ya dogara ne kan fahimtar cewa gudummawar da aka samu don samar da dashen zai iya amfanar da buƙatun lafiyar iyalin mai bayarwa. Idan mun shirya karɓar dasshe idan muna da buƙatar hakan, to ya kamata mu kasance a shirye don bayarwa Ɗa'a: Ƙa'idodin Jagora na WHO kan Dashen Tantanin halitta da Tishu da Gaɓoɓin Jiki

translations.source: In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.
translations.reference: A 1987, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta arba'in, ta damu da kasuwanci don riba kan sassan jikin mutum, ta fara shirye-shiryen Ƙa'idodin Jagora na farko na WHO game da dashe, wanda Majalisar ta amince da shi a 1991 a ƙuduri na WHA44.25. Waɗannan Ƙa'idodin Jagora sun yi tasiri sosai ga ƙa'idodin ƙwararru da ayyuka da kuma dokoki a duk duniya cikin kusan shekaru ashirin. Bayan tuntuɓa wadda ta ɗauki shekaru da yawa, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta Sittin da Uku ta amince da ƙuduri na WHA63.22 a ranar 21 ga Mayu 2010, tare da amincewa da sabunta Ƙa'idodin Jagororanci na WHO da gano wuraren ci gaba don inganta ayyukan ba da gudummawa da dasawa. Sanarwar Istanbul Ƙwararru na ba da gudummawa da dasawa daga dukan yankuna, ta hanyar ƙungiyoyi da cibiyoyi da yawa, suna goyon bayan Sanarwar Istanbul game da fataucin sassan jiki da yawon shaƙatawa da aka bunƙasa a ƙarƙashin jagorancin ƙungiyar dashe da kuma Ƙungiya Nephrology ta Ƙasa-da-ƙasa a watan Mayun 2008.

translations.source: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year.
translations.reference: Tafiya da lafiya Taƙaitaccen Bayani: Tafiya tsakanin ƙasashe na iya barazana na haɗari daban-daban ga lafiya, wanda ya danganta da yanayin matafiyin da ita tafiyar. Matafiya na iya haɗuwa da mahimman canje-canje a lokaci guda a cikin ladabi da yanayin zafi da ƙwayoyin halitta da zafin jiki waɗanda zasu iya haifar da rashin lafiya. Bugu da ƙari, munanan haɗarurruka ga lafiya na iya fitowa a cikin wuraren da babu wadata da ingancin tsaftar jiki da muhalli, ayyukan likitanci ba a tsara su da kyau ba haka kuma babu tsaftataccen ruwa. Tafiya tsakanin ƙasashe na ƙaruwa, yadda ya zama miliyoyin mutane ke tafiya don aiki, zamantakewa, nishaɗi da kuma dalilai na aikin jin ƙaidalilai kowace shekara.

translations.source: All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:
translations.reference: Dole ne dukkanin matafiya su shirya don nau'ikan haɗari ga lafiya da za a iya samu cikin yanayin da ba a sani ba a kafin da yayin da kuma bayan tafiya. Ya kamata mutane da ke shirin tafiya su nemi shawara kan haɗarin da za su iya samu a wurin da zasu je kuma su fahimci hanyar da zasu kare lafiyarsu da kuma yadda zasu rage haɗarin kamuwa da cuta. Shirin gaba sune, matakan da suka dace da taka tsantsan na iya kare lafiyar matafiya, kuma suna rage haɗarin haɗari da samun cuta. Kodayake masana'antar likitanci da masana'antar tafiye-tafiye na iya samar da taimako mai yawa da kuma shawara kyau, ya kasance da alhakin matafiya ne neman bayanai da fahimtar haɗarurrukan da ke ƙunshe da kuma ɗaukan matakan kariya da suka dace don kiyaye lafiyarsu yayin tafiya. Haɗari: Yayin tantance haɗarurrukan lafiya na tafiya ƙasashen a duniya, ya kamata matafiya da ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya suyi la'akari da waɗannan manyan abubuwan da za a kawo:

**Yawancin masu tafiya daidai dole ne su yi auna da yau daɗɗa game da haɗarin lafiyayensu da za su iya samunsu a wata al'ada sabuwar wuta kafiya, a lokaci da kuma bayan tafiya. Mutum waɗanda ke ci gaba da yi aure da yi tafiya da yawa dole ne su yi binciken koyaushe game da haɗarin da ke a wuraren da suka zauna da suka zaba, kuma su fahimci yadda su ci gaba da kare lafiyarsu da rage yaduwar kwayoyin cututtuka. Auna da fatara, hanyoyin hana kwayoyin cututtuka da yawa, da kiyashi da kiyashi za su ci gaba da kare lafiyar masu tafiya da rage yaduwar kwayoyin cututtuka da lalata. translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided text into Hausa, aiming for clarity and naturalness within the language:

**Translation:**

*   **Mode of transport:** *Naïjarki* (meaning vehicle, transport)
*   **Destination(s):** *Gurin shiga* (meaning place of destination)
*   **Duration and season of travel:** *Lokacin da kuma maki na safarar* (meaning the time and season of the journey)
*   **Purpose of travel:** *Amun mutuncin safara* (meaning the reason for the journey)
*   **Standards of accommodation, food hygiene and sanitation:** *Matsalolin gidaje, dabbanci, da kuma daudauwar lafiya* (meaning standards of lodging, food safety, and cleanliness)

**Full Translated Text:**

Naïjarki, gururin shiga, lokacin da kuma maki na safarar, amun mutuncin safara, matsalolin gidaje, dabbanci, da kuma daudauwar lafiya.

---

**Notes on the Translation:**

*   **Hausa.gs:**  I've assumed you're referring to a specific dialect or style of Hausa.  Hausa has regional variations. This translation leans towards a more general, widely understood form.
*   **Word Choice:** I've tried to use common and easily understood Hausa words.
*   **Grammar:** Hausa grammar is different from English. I've structured the translation to be grammatically correct in Hausa.

translations.source: behaviour of the traveller underlying health of the traveller Travellers may encounter the following health risks depending on their destination: animal and insect bites, including mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, and some flies, which can spread diseases such as malaria exposure to blood or other bodily fluids
translations.reference: halayyar matafiyi Yanayin lafiyar matafiyi Matafiya na iya riskar waɗannan haɗurran ga lafiya dangane da wurin da suke zuwa: cizon dabbobi da na ƙwari, wanda ya ƙunshi sauro da tantana da ƙwari masu shan jini da sauran ƙwari da zasu iya yaɗa cuta kamar zazzaɓin sauro haɗuwa da jini ko wasu ruwa na jiki
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - likely referring to a Google Translate format), aiming for accuracy and natural-sounding Hausa:

**Translation:**

“Ƙungiyar masu tafiya daidai da lafiyata na masu tafiya.  Masu tafiya za su iya zanta matsalar lafiya mai-ruwan sama da yawa bisa wuraren da suka yi niyyar zuwa.  Hassasa da ciwon dabbobi da juna, kamar mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, da wasu nauyi, wanda za su iya yi daidai da lalata kwayoyin cututtuka kamar malaria.  Wasa da ruwan jiki ko wasu ruwan jiki na mutuwan.”

**Explanation of Choices & Nuances:**

* **“Ƙungiyar masu tafiya”** -  This translates to "The group of travelers" or "Travelers" - a common way to start a sentence about travelers.
* **“daidai da lafiyata na masu tafiya”** - This translates to "underlying health of the travelers" - I've interpreted this as referring to the overall health status of the travelers.
* **“Masu tafiya za su iya zanta matsalar lafiya mai-ruwan sama da yawa”** - This translates to "Travellers may encounter the following health risks" -  "Zanta" means "to encounter" or "to face."  "Mai-ruwan sama da yawa" means "many" and "serious" or "significant."
* **“Hassasa da ciwon dabbobi da juna”** - This translates to "animal and insect bites" - "Hassasa" means "exposed to" or "encountering."
* **“kamar mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, da wasu nauyi”** - This translates to "including mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, and some flies" -  "Kamara" means "like" or "such as."
* **“wanda za su iya yi daidai da lalata kwayoyin cututtuka kamar malaria”** - This translates to "which can spread diseases such as malaria" - "Wanda" means "which" or "that." "Yi daidai da lalata" means "to spread" or "to transmit."
* **“Wasa da ruwan jiki ko wasu ruwan jiki na mutuwan.”** - This translates to "exposure to blood or other bodily fluids."  "Wasa da" means "exposure to." "Ruwan jiki na mutuwan" means "bodily fluids of a person" (literally "blood of a person").

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

* **Context is Key:** Hausa, like many low-resource languages, relies heavily on context.  A more detailed understanding of the document's purpose would allow for a more precise translation.
* **Multiple Valid Translations:**  There are often multiple ways to express the same idea in Hausa.  I've aimed for a common and understandable phrasing.
* **Regional Variations:** Hausa has regional dialects. This translation is generally understandable across most of the Hausa-speaking areas.

translations.source: Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most dangerous, for which no vaccines exist. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.
translations.reference: Hakanan matafiya zasu iya haɗuwa da cututtuka da dama, kuma da yawa daga cikin waɗannan za a iya daƙile su ta hanyar alurar rigakafi. Duk da haka, akwai wasu cututtuka, ciki har da wasu daga cikin mafiya haɗari, waɗanda ba rigakafinsu. Wuraren da inda masauƙi ya ke da tsafta da aikin kiwon lafiya da ruwa mai kyau na da ƙarancin barazana ga lafiyar matafiya. Inda ba a samar da waɗannan ayyukan ba, ya kamata matafiya su ɗauki tsattsauran matakan kariya don gujewa rashin lafiya. Ya kamata a dauƙi gargaɗi bisa tafiya daga majiyoyin gwamnati da mahimmanci.

translations.source: Prevention: Before travel: contact a travel medicine centre or a physician, preferably 4–8 weeks before departure to receive any required vaccinations; read up on local diseases related to your destination; request information on malaria risk and prevention of mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate preventive medication and packing a bednet and insect repellent; get a medical and dental check-up;
translations.reference: Yin rigakafi: Kafin tafiya: tuntuɓi cibiyar magungunan matafiya ko likita, mafi dacewa, makonni 4-8 kafin tafiya don karɓar duk wani rigakafi da ake buƙata; Yi karatu kan cututtukan gida masu alaƙa da wurin da zaka je; nemi bayani game da haɗarin zazzabin cizon sauro da rigakafin cizon sauro. Wannan na iya ƙunsar sayan magani na kariya da tattara sangen gado mai kwarar ƙwari; yi binciken lafiyar jiki da na haƙori;

translations.source: obtain prescriptions for medication according to length of stay and assemble a suitable medical kit; purchase medical insurance with appropriate cover abroad During travel: eat only thoroughly cooked food and drink only bottled or packaged cold drinks. Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful.
translations.reference: samu takardar magani bisa tsawon zama ka kuma tattara duk magungunan da suka dace; sayi inshorar kiwon lafiya da duk abin da ya dace a ƙasar waje A lokacin tafiya: Ku ci abincin da aka dafa sosai kaɗai da abin sha wanda aka sanya a cikin kwalba mai sanyi. A tafasa ruwan sha idan akwai shakku akan tsaftarsa.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - likely referring to a specific Hausa script or dialect), aiming for clarity and natural-sounding language:

**Hausa Translation:**

"Hankwaci karar matawun mai ruwan shan da ya fi yawan zama, kuma kammala kayobin likirtaka na likita. Sayen保険 mai kyau wanda ya yi aiki a wajen wani kasa.

A lokacin da muke yi jana’izar: Sai mun yi amfani da abin da ke da lafiya kuma mun yi amfani da mabuɗi ko mabuɗi da kyananɗi.  Sai mu yiɗaya mabuɗi mai ruwan daɗɗa idan ana son kiyaye lafiyata."

**Explanation of Choices and Nuances:**

*   **"Hankwaci karar matawun mai ruwan shan"** -  "Obtain prescriptions for medication" - This translates to "Getting the prescription for the medicine."
*   **"da ya fi yawan zama"** - "according to length of stay" -  "That is longer than the length of stay."
*   **"kammala kayobin likirtaka na likita"** - "assemble a suitable medical kit" - "Complete the medical kit supplies."
*   **"Sayen保険 mai kyau wanda ya yi aiki a wani kasa"** - "purchase medical insurance with appropriate cover abroad" - "Buy a good insurance that works in another country." (Note: "保険" is a loanword for "insurance".)
*   **"A lokacin da muke yi jana’izar"** - "During travel" - "When we are traveling."
*   **"Sai mun yi amfani da abin da ke da lafiya kuma mun yi amfani da mabuɗi ko mabuɗi da kyananɗi"** - "eat only thoroughly cooked food and drink only bottled or packaged cold drinks." -  This is a more descriptive way to convey the caution about food and drink.  "Mabuɗi" means food, and "Mabuɗi da kyananɗi" means packaged food.
*   **"Sai mu yiɗaya mabuɗi mai ruwan daɗɗa idan ana son kiyaye lafiyata."** - "Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful." - "Boil the water if you want to keep your health."

**Important Notes:**

*   **Hausa is a diverse language:**  There are regional variations in Hausa. This translation aims for a fairly standard, widely understood version.
*   **Context is Key:**  Without more context about the intended audience (e.g., travelers, healthcare professionals), it's difficult to make absolutely perfect choices.

Would you like me to:

*   Translate a different document?
*   Adjust this translation based on a specific region of Hausa?
*   Explain any part of the translation in more detail?

translations.source: be aware of accidents or problems related to traffic, animals, allergies, sun and sport; carry a card showing your blood group; wear a medical alert bracelet, if you have allergies or a chronic disease Travellers should obtain a medical examination on their return if they: return with a fever from a country where malaria is or may be present
translations.reference: Yi hankali da haɗurra ko matsalolin da suka danganci zirga-zirga da dabbob da rashin lafiyan da rana da wasanni; ku ɗauki katin da ke nuna rukunin jininku; Saka sarƙar da ake nuna alama idan kuna da rashin lafiyan cuta ko cuta mai tsanani Ya kamata matafiya suyi bincike kan lafiyarsu bayan sun dawo idan suka: Dawo daga wata ƙasa tare da zazzaɓi inda akwai zazzaɓin cizon sauro ko ana iya samu
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - likely referring to a specific Hausa script or format), aiming for clarity and natural-sounding language:

**Hausa Translation:**

“Yaya da abu da ke tana da lalata ko matsale-mata game da taso-tasoshin kasa, dabbobi, lalata, rana da gasasshihon; da kuma yi daidai da katin da yake nuna mai-gishiri na jiki.  Yi amfani da sallar shiri, idan kuna da lalata ko kasuwa mai kauri.  Masifta biyu da ke da laishe biyu dole ne su yi binciken laishe a yayansu na komawa, idan su koma da zunsa daga wata gaba daya inda kwaya ke da shi ko za ta kasance.”

**Explanation of Choices and Nuances:**

* **“Yaya da abu da ke tana da lalata…”** –  This translates to "Be aware of things that may be dangerous..."  It’s a common and polite way to start a warning.
* **“tasoshin kasa”** – “traffic” –  A direct translation.
* **“dabbobi”** – “animals”
* **“lalata”** – “allergies” –  A standard Hausa word.
* **“rana”** – “sun”
* **“gasasshihon”** – “sport” –  A direct translation.
* **“da kuma yi daidai da katin da yake nuna mai-gishiri na jiki”** – “and also carry a card showing your blood group.”  This is a more detailed explanation.
* **“Yi amfani da sallar shiri”** – “Wear a medical alert bracelet.”  “Sallar shiri” literally means “bracelet of the wrist.”
* **“idanna kuna da lalata ko kasuwa mai kauri”** – “if you have allergies or a chronic disease.” “Kasuwa mai kauri” is a common way to express a chronic illness.
* **“Masifta biyu da ke da laishe biyu dole ne su yi binciken laishe a yayansu na komawa…”** – “Travellers should obtain a medical examination on their return…”  This is a more formal phrasing.
* **“idana su koma da zunsa daga wata gaba daya inda kwaya ke da shi ko za ta kasance.”** – “if they return with a fever from a country where malaria is or may be present.”  This is a clear explanation of the medical recommendation.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

* **Context is Key:** Hausa, like many low-resource languages, has many regional variations.  The best translation will depend on the specific region where it will be used.
* **Naturalness:** I've aimed for a translation that sounds natural to a Hausa speaker.  A literal word-for-word translation often doesn't work well.
* **Cultural Sensitivity:**  The phrasing is designed to be respectful and appropriate for the target audience.

To help me refine this translation further, could you tell me:

*   **What is the intended audience for this translation?** (e.g., general public, healthcare professionals, specific age group?)
*   **Is there a particular region of Hausa you're targeting?** (e.g., Northern Nigeria, Niger, etc.)

**ملاحظات:**

*   **الأسلوب:**  هذا الترجمة تهدف إلى أن تكون طبيعية وواضفي.  إذا كان هناك حاجة إلى أسلوب معين (على سبيل المثال، رسمي أو غير رسمي)، فيرجى إخباري.
*   **الكلمات:**  إذا كان هناك أي كلمات أو عبارات معينة تريدني أن أستخدمها أو أتجنبها، فيرجى إخباري.
*   **التعقيد:**  إذا كان النص الأصلي معقدًا، فقد يكون من الضروري تعديل الترجمة لضمان الوضوح.

يرجى إخباري إذا كنت تريد مني أن أقوم بتعديل الترجمة أو إذا كان لديك أي أسئلة.

translations.source: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB).
translations.reference: Tarin fuka Taƙaitaccen bayani: Ƙwayar cuta mai suna Mycobacterium tuberculosis ce ke haifar da cutar tarin fuka, kuma mafi yawan lokaci tana shafar huhu. Tarin fuka na yaɗuwa ta hanyar iska idan mutane masu ƙwayar cutar a huhunsu suka yi tar, attishawa ko tofar da yawu. Mutum na buƙatar shaƙar ƙwayoyin cutar kaɗan ne kawai kafin ya kamu da ita. A kowace shekara, mutane miliyan 10 ne ke kamuwa da cutar tarin fuka(TB).

translations.source: Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.reference: Duk da kasancewarta cuta da za a iya karewa kuma ana iya warkewa, mutane miliyan 1.5 suna mutuwa daga tarin fuka a kowace shekara - wanda ya sa ta zama babbar cuta mai yaɗuwa da ke kisa a duniya. Tarin fuka na kan gaba a jerin sanadin mutuwar mutane masu cuta mai karyar garkuwar jiki HIV, kuma yana samar da kaso mai yawa na cutuka masu ƙin jin magani Yawancin masu kamuwa da cutar tarin fuka na rayuwa a ƙasashe masu ƙaranci da matsakaicin ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi, amma ana samun TB a ko'ina a duniya. Ana iya samun kusan rabin dukkan masu fama da tarin fuka a kasashe 8: Bangladesh, China, Indiya, Indonesiya, Najeriya, Pakistan, Philippines da Afirka ta Kudu. An ƙiyasta cewa kimanin kaso ɗaya bisa huɗu na al'ummar duniya na kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar TB, sai dai yawancin mutane ba za su kama cutar ta tarin fuka ba, wasu kuma jikinsu na yaƙar cutar.

translations.source: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others.
translations.reference: Waɗanda ke ɗauke da ƙwayar cutar amma ba su kamu da rashin lafiya ba tukunna ba sa iya yaɗa ta. Mutanen da suka kamu da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka na da hatsarin tsawon rayuwa na kaso 5 zuwa 10 na kamuwa da cutar ta TB. Mutanen da ke da raunin garkuwar jiki, kamar mutanen da ke rayuwa da cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki wato HIV, ƙaranci abinci mai gina jiki ko ciwon suga, ko masu shan sigari, sun fi zama cikin hatsarin yin rashin lafiyar. Alamomi: Alamomin cutar tarin fuka sun haɗa da: Taro na lokaci mai tsawo, Ciwon ƙirji, Kasala ko gajiya, Ramewa, Zazzaɓi, Gumin dare. Mafi yawan lokuta, waɗannan alamomi sukan kasance marasa tsanani na tsawon watanni, wanda hakan ke haifar da jinkiri wajen neman lafiya da kuma ƙara hatsarin yaɗa cutar ga wasu.
translations.hypothesis:

**Hausa Translation:**

“Wasa da ya shafi kwaya da ciyawa da kuma a yanzu ba ya jima ba, ba za su iya harrastawa shi ba.  Matattawa da suka ci gajiyar da kwayar TB, suna da kyakkyawar kashi 5-10% na rayuwansu suna jima da TB.  Wasu masu lalata da kwayar jan zazzabi, kamar wadanda ke da HIV, lalata, lalata ko diabetes, ko wadanda ke amfani da tabbas, suna da yuwarsa ta fi na wasu mai girma na jima.  Lamarin da ke faruwa:  Alakaren zangewa na TB sun yi daidai da:  Zangewa mai tsawon makwanciya, lalata da kwayar da ke a cikin kwayar ruwan koda, lalata, lalata, zagin, da lalata da kwayar da ke a cikin kwayar ruwan koda.  Lafiya da ke da shi da kwayar da ke a cikin kwayar ruwan koda, da kuma lalata da kwayar da ke a cikin kwayar ruwan koda, za su daidaita da yawan zangewa da yawan harrastawa da wani wani, wanda ke da yawa na harrastawa da kwayar da ke a cikin kwayar ruwan koda da wani wani.”

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

* **"Wasa da ya shafi kwaya da ciyawa"**:  This translates to "Those who are infected but not (yet) ill."  I've aimed for a clear and common way to express this concept.
* **"Matattawa da suka ci gajiyar da kwayar TB"**:  "People infected with TB bacteria."
* **"Kyakkyawar kashi 5-10%"**: "5-10% lifetime risk."
* **"Masu lalata da kwayar jan zazzabi"**: “People with compromised immune systems.”
* **"Wadanda ke da HIV, lalata, lalata ko diabetes, ko wadanda ke amfani da tabbas"**:  This translates the list of risk factors.  I've used common Hausa terms for these conditions.
* **"Lamarin da ke faruwa"**: “Symptoms.”
* **"Alakaren zangewa na TB"**: “Common symptoms of TB disease.”
* **I’ve tried to maintain a natural flow and use vocabulary that is generally understood in Hausa-speaking communities.**

**Important Notes:**

* **Hausa is a highly contextual language.**  The best translation can depend on the specific audience and the context in which the translation will be used.

translations.source: If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.reference: Idan ma'aikacin lafiya ya yi zargin mara lafiya na fama a cutar tarin fuka, zai aika wannan marar lafiyar domin yin gwaji. Ta ɓangaren cutar tarin fuka da ta shafi huhu, za a buƙaci mara lafiya da ya ba da yawunsa don a yi gwajin cutar ta tarin fuka. Ga tarin fuka da bai shafi huhu ba kuma, ana iya gwada samfurin ruwan jiki ko ko wani ɓangare da cutar ta shafa. WHO na ba da shawara yin gwajin gano ƙwayoyin cuta cikin sauri a matsayin gwajin farko ga mutanen da ke nuna alamun kamuwa da tarin fuka. Sauran hanyoyin gwaje-gwajen na iya haɗawa da duba yawu a ƙarƙashin madubin binciken ƙwaƙwaf da kuma hoton ƙirji.

translations.source: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable.
translations.reference: Wajen kamuwa da tarin fuka, mutum na gamuwa da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka wacce ta zama a jiki ba tare da wani tasiri ba. Kamuwa da wannan ƙwayar cutar na iya juyewa ya zama cikakkiyar cutar tarin fuka idan garkuwar jikinsu ta samu rauni. Ta yiwu mutanen da suka kamu da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka ba za su nuna wasu alamomin cutar ta tarin fuka ba. Don gano ko an kamu da cutar tarin fuka, ma'aikatan lafiya za su duba marasa lafiya da ke cikin haɗari don kawar da yiwuwar kamuwa da tarin fuka, kuma suna iya amfani da gwajin fata ko jini don bincikar tarin fuka. Magani: Tarin fuka cuta ce da ake iya warkewa.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - representing a standard Hausa script):

**Translation:**

"Wannan zai faru inda mutum ya shiga jikkara da jinkirikin TB.  Jinkirikin na TB na yi tsalle a jikinmu kamar mai tashi, amma bai yi aiki ba.  Idan kwakwan jiki na mutum ya yi zagi, to jinkirin na TB zai iya zama kasar TB.  Matan da jinkirin na TB ba za su dawo da alama ko rauni na kasar TB ba. Don gauran jinkirin na TB, masu kula da lafiya za su gwamanta masu cututtaru don gujere kasar TB, kuma za su yi binciken jari ko yanci na jiksa don gauran jinkirin na TB.  **Matakan ginawa:** Kasar TB na iya jiyarsa."

**Explanation of some choices and considerations:**

*   **"Wannan zai faru inda..."** - This is a common way to start a sentence explaining a situation or process in Hausa.
*   **"Jinkirin na TB"** -  This is the standard Hausa term for "TB infection."
*   **"Mai tashi"** -  This translates to "lying dormant" or "inactive" and is a good way to convey the idea of the bacteria being inactive.
*   **"Kwakwan jiki ya yi zagi"** - This translates to "their immune system weakens"
*   **"Masu kula da lafiya"** - This translates to "healthcare providers."
*   **"Gwamananta masu cututtaru"** - This translates to "screen at-risk patients."
*   **"Guairewa"** - This translates to "rule out"
*   **"Yanci na jiksa"** - This translates to "skin or blood test."
*   **"Matakan ginawa"** - This translates to "Treatment"
*   **"Jiyarsa"** - This translates to "is curable."

**Important Note:**  Hausa has many dialects and variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable and standard form of Hausa.  For highly specialized or formal contexts, a different phrasing might be preferred.

translations.source: It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.
translations.reference: Ana magance ta ta hanyar shan daidaitattun magunguna kala 4 na tsawon wata 6. Magungunan da ake yawan amfani da su sun haɗa da rifampicin da isoniazid. Amma wasu lokutan cutar tarin fuka ba ta karɓar daidaitattun magungunan. A wannan yanayin, mara lafiyan yana fama da tarin fuka da ba ya jin magani. Tarin fuka da ba ya jin magani na ɗaukar lokaci mai tsawo kuma yana da sarƙaƙiya.

translations.source: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time.
translations.reference: Ana ba wa marar lafiyar maganin tarin fuka da yake buƙata tare da bayanai da kuma sa'ido da tallafi daga ma'aikacin lafiya ko wani mai aikin sa kai da aka horas. Idan babu tallafi, tabbatar da an kammala shan maganin na iya zama abu mai wahala. Idan ba a kammala shan maganin yadda ya kamata ba, cutar na iya juyewa zuwa wacce ba ta jin magani kuma ta yaɗu. Game da kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka kuwa (ta yadda mara lafiyar ya kamu da ƙwayar cutar amma bai fara rashin lafiya ba), ana iya ba shi maganin kariya daga cutar tarin fuka don hana bayyanar alamomin cutar. Maganin iri ɗaya ake sha da na magance cutar, amma na lokacin da bai kai wancan tsawo ba.

Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - representing a standard Hausa script), aiming for clarity and natural-sounding language:

**Hausa Translation:**

“Wasu matuka da kaya don jawar da ciwon jini (TB) na watsa wa marigayi da aminci, aiki da kiyashi, kuma gobe. Idan ba a yi wannan aiki ba, zai iya zana ciwon jini, kuma zai iya yi daidai. Idan ciwon jini ya koma wani wace wasa (indai kuma marigayi bai kashe ba), za a iya ba shi da kaya mai gwanin ciwon jini don ciwon jini ya koma wani wace wasa. Kaya na watsa wa marigayi da kaya na watsa da wani wace wasa da wani wace wasa.”

**Explanation of Choices and Nuances:**

*   **“Wasu matuka da kaya don jawar da ciwon jini (TB)”** -  "Some drugs are provided for the treatment of tuberculosis (TB)."  I've included "TB" in parentheses for clarity, as it's a common abbreviation.
*   **“na watsa wa marigayi da aminci, aiki da kiyashi, kuma gobe.”** - “to give the patient information, supervision, and support.”  This breaks down the English into more natural Hausa phrasing.  “Watsa” means to give or provide. “Aminci” means confidence or reassurance. “Aiki da kiyashi” means supervision. “Kuma gobe” means and support.
*   **“Idan ba a yi wannan aiki ba, zai iya zana ciwon jini, kuma zai iya yi daidai.”** - “Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult.”  “Zai iya zana” means it can become. “Kuma zai iya yi daidai” means and can spread.
*   **“Idan ciwon jini ya koma wani wace wasa (indai kuma marigayi bai kashe ba)”** - “In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill).”  “Ya koma wani wace wasa” means it can become another form. “Indai kuma marigayi bai kashe ba” means and the patient is not ill.
*   **“za a iya ba shi da kaya mai gwanin ciwon jini don ciwon jini ya koma wani wace wasa.”** - “TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease.” “Za a iya ba shi da kaya mai gwanin ciwon jini” means it can be given preventive treatment. “Don ciwon jini ya koma wani wace wasa” means to stop the onset of disease.
*   **“Kaya na watsa da wani wace wasa da wani wace wasa.”** - “This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time.”  This is a simplified way to convey the idea of a shorter duration.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Hausa, like many low-resource languages, relies heavily on context.  The best translation will depend on the specific audience and the purpose of the document.
*   **Cultural Sensitivity:**  It's crucial to ensure the translation is culturally appropriate and avoids any potentially offensive language.
*   **Review by Native Speakers:**  Ideally, this translation should be reviewed and refined by native Hausa speakers to ensure accuracy and naturalness.

translations.source: Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.
translations.reference: Zaɓuɓɓukan magani na baya-bayan nan sun ƙara gajarce tsawon lokacin shan maganin zuwa wata 1 ko 3 kawai, idan aka kwatanta da watanni 6 da akan yi a baya.

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate):**

*   “Ƙarin ” – Ka-rin (like “car-in”)

*   “ hanya ta kasa da ” – han-yaar ta ka-sa da (han-yah-ar ta ka-sa da)
*   “ shehu daya ko ” – shehu da-ya ko (shehu da-ya ko)
*   “ uku kwasan shiga ” – uku kwa-san shi-ga (uku kwa-san shi-ga)
*   “ kamar yadda ke a baya da ” – ka-mar ya-da-da ke ba-ya da (ka-mar ya-da-da ke ba-ya da)

**Explanation of Choices:**

*   “shehu daya ko uku kwasan shiga” – This translates to “1 or 3 months”. “Kwasan shiga” is a common way to express months.
