translations.source: unequal power relationships between men and women; social norms that decrease education and paid employment opportunities; an exclusive focus on women’s reproductive roles; and potential or actual experience of physical, sexual and emotional violence. While poverty is an important barrier to positive health outcomes for both men and women, poverty tends to yield a higher burden on women and girls’ health due to, for example, feeding practices (malnutrition) and use of unsafe cooking fuels (COPD).
translations.reference: bambancin iko da ke akwai tsakanin maza da mata; Tsarin zamantakewa da ke daƙile ilimi da batun damarmaki na aikin da za a biya mutum; Ba wa ɓangaren batun rawar mata wajen haihuwa muhimmanci; da da kuma fuskantar cin zarafi na sarari ko na fannin saduwa ko na imoshan. Duk da talauci na taka rawa wajen kawo cikas ga samun ingantaccen kiwon lafiya ga maza da mata; mata da ƙananan yara mata sun fi fuskantar ƙalubalen talauci, ga misali ɓangaren cin abinci (rashin wadataccen abinci) da amfani da abin girki masu illa (COPD).

translations.source: Yaws (Endemic treponematoses) Overview: Yaws is a chronic skin infection characterized by papillomas (noncancerous lumps) and ulcers. It is caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum subspecies pertenue, which belongs to the same group of bacteria that causes venereal syphilis. Yaws primarily affects children aged under 15 years who live in poor communities in warm, humid and tropical forested areas of Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Pacific islands. The majority of affected populations live rural areas, far from health services.
translations.reference: cutar fata (Endemic treponematoses) Taƙaitaccen bayani: cutar fata ta Yaws cutar fata ce mai tsananin gaske da ke da yanayi na kumburi da gyambo (ciwo). Bakteriyar da ake kira Treponema Pallidum ce ke haifar da wannan cutar fata wacce aka sanya ta cikin kashin baktariyar da ke haifar da sifilis da ake samu ta hanyar saduwa. cutar fata ta Yaws ta fi shafar yara 'yan ƙasa da shekaru 15 da ke rayuwa cikin talauci a wuri mai yanayin zafi a yankin Afirka da Asiya da Latin America da tsibirin Fasifik. Mafi yawan waɗanda ke kamuwa da cutar na rayuwa ne a ƙauyuka da ke da nisa da wuraren kiwon lafiya.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs), aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the context:

**Yawu (Cutkta na Treponema mai cututtuka) – Bayani:** Yawu shine cutta mai tsalle da ke da lalata, wanda ke da kwayoyi (kwararfin da ba abu na cututtucewa) da lalatas.  Tana akayi da kwayar cutta ta Treponema pallidum, wanda ke da nauki na pertenue, wanda ke karkashin gungun kwayarkuwa da ke akayi da cuttan venereal syphilis. Yawu na shirin akayi da masu kare-shegi da ke tsawon shekaru mai kamance-camance, da suka wuce 15, da ke zaune a iyalaa-alujin da ke da halin zangwaza, a wuraren da ke da ruwan daidai da yanayin ruwan daidai da yanayin yan kasa da yan kasa na Afrika, Asia, Amurika ta Arewa da Jihar Pacific.  Manyakan masu fama da cuttan na zaune a wuraren shaguna, da yawa da yawan aikin hafsassa.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

* **Yawu (Cutkta na Treponema mai cututtuka):**  "Yawu" is the Hausa word for Yaws.  "Cutkta na Treponema mai cututtuka" is a more descriptive translation of "Endemic treponematoses," explaining it as a skin infection caused by Treponema.
* **Bayani:**  "Overview" is translated as "Bayani" which means "information" or "explanation."
* **Kwayoyi (kwararfin da ba abu na cututtucewa):**  "Papillomas" are translated as "kwayoyi" (lumps) and then further clarified as "kwararfin da ba abu na cututtucewa" (non-cancerous lumps).
* **Wuraren da ke da halin zangwaza:** "Poor communities" is translated as "Wuraren da ke da halin zangwaza" (areas with a difficult situation).
* **Wuraren shaguna:** "Rural areas" is translated as "Wuraren shaguna" (areas far from the town).
* **Manyakan masu fama da cuttan:** "The majority of affected populations" is translated as "Manyakan masu fama da cuttan" (many people affected by the disease).

**Important Note:**  Hausa has many regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable version.  For highly specialized or formal contexts, a local expert in Hausa medical terminology would be ideal.

translations.source: Poverty, low socio-economic conditions and poor personal hygiene facilitate the spread of yaws. Although there are over 80 000 cases of yaws each year, experts believe the disease can be controlled and ultimately eradicated for several reasons. First, it only occurs in humans, not animals, making control much easier. It is also easy to treat with readily available drugs and has already been eliminated in some countries, including India. The remaining pockets of yaws infection, although usually in remote places, means further spread is less likely with proper surveillance and control measures.
translations.reference: Talauci da yanayi na rashin abin hannu da rashin tsabta ne ke taimakawa wajen yaɗuwar cutar fata ta Yaws. duk da akwai rahotanni 80000 na kamuwa da cutar fata ta Yaws a duk shekara, masana sun yi amanna cewa za a iya magance cutar da ma kawar da ita saboda da dalilai masu yawa. Da farko dai ana samun cutar ne a cikin bil'Adama ba dabba ba wanda ke nuna sauƙin magance cutar. Za a iya magance cutar cikin sauƙi ta hanyar amfani da magunguna da tuni akwai su kamar yadda aka yi nasarar kawar da cutar a wasu ƙasashe kamar Indiya. Wasu 'yan ƙalilan da ke da cutar na zaune a keɓantattun wurare wanda hakan ke nuna sauƙin daƙile yaɗuwarta idana aka yi amfani da matakai na bibiya da kulawa.

translations.source: Symptoms: There are two stages of yaws infection. The early stage is when the patient is infectious and can spread the disease to others. In this stage, a papule (a noncancerous, outward-growing lump) develops at the site of infection. This papule is full of the organisms and may persist for 3–6 months followed by natural healing. Without treatment, this is followed by disseminated skin lesions over the body.
translations.reference: Alamomin kamuwa: Akwai matakai biyu na kwamuwa da cutar fata ta Yaws. mataki na farko shi ne matakin da mai larurar ke ɗauke da cutar kuma zai iya yaɗawa ga sauran mutane. a wannan mataki a kan samu ɓullowar ƙurji (wanda bai shafi kansa ba da ke ɓullowa kamar ƙurji) wanda ke fara girma da zarar mutum ya kamu da shi. ƙurjin yana dauke da ƙwayar cutar da kuma kan ɗauki watanni 3-6 ana maganinsa. Idan kuma ba a ɗauki mataki na magani ba, yana cigaba da yaɗuwa ne a kan fatar zuwa sauran sassan jiki.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided text into Hausa (gs - representing a standard Hausa script):

**Translation:**

Tsawarku: Akwai dami biyu na cututtacin yaws. Damina ta biyu shine lokacin da kwari yana da kwaya kuma zai iya harrtsawa matsalar ta ga wasu mutane. A wannan damina, wani papule (wanda shine kauri mai lalata, wanda keauka) ya kasance a wajannin lalacewar. Wannan papule yana ciki da jinin hanyoyin da ke ajiya kuma zai ci gaba da tsawon makwanciya zuwa 3-6 wata kuma a nan daɗa ya yi sauki da kyakkyau. Ko da ba a kula da shi ba, wannan yana daidai da lalata da jini a kan jikin.

**Explanation of Choices & Notes:**

*   **Tsawarku:**  This translates to "Symptoms."
*   **Damina:** This translates to "stages" or "phases."
*   **Kwari:** This translates to "organisms" or "bacteria" (in the context of a disease).
*   **Da kwaya:** This translates to "infectious."
*   **Harrtsawa:** This translates to "spread."
*   **Papule:**  I've kept "papule" as is, as it's a medical term that doesn't have a direct Hausa equivalent.  I've added a descriptive phrase to explain it.
*   **Kauri mai lalata:** This translates to "noncancerous, outward-growing lump."
*   **Auwa ka:** This translates to "full of."
*   **Tsawon makwanciya:** This translates to "3–6 months."
*   **Sauki da kyakkyau:** This translates to "natural healing."
*   **Lalata da jini:** This translates to "disseminated skin lesions" (literally "spreading of blood/skin").

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Medical translations *always* require careful consideration of context.  I've aimed for accuracy, but a medical professional fluent in both English and Hausa should review this for final confirmation.
*   **Terminology:** Medical terminology can be challenging.  There might be more precise Hausa terms available depending on the specific area of medicine.
*   **Readability:**  I've tried to make the translation clear and understandable for a general audience.

translations.source: Bone pain and bone lesions may also occur. The second stage is noninfectious and typically appears five years after infection. It is characterized by disabling consequences of the nose, bones and palmar/plantar hyperkeratosis (thickening). After a field diagnosis based on these symptoms, the disease can be confirmed by examining a sample from a skin legion under a special type of microscope (darkfield examination). Blood tests are not typically used because yaws is closely related to the bacterium that causes syphilis and the two diseases will show the same result.
translations.reference: ana iya fara jin raɗaɗi daga cikin ƙashi da musayar lafiyayyen ƙashi da wani. mataki na biyu shi ne matakin da cutar ba za ta iya yaɗuwa ba wanda hakan na faruwa ne kusan shekaru biyar da kamuwa da cutar. ana iya fayyace cutar da ƙandarewar hanci da ƙashi da tafin hannu/dunduniya (kaurara). bayan nazarin alamomin cutar, za a iya tabbatar da cutar ne kawai idan aka haska fatar da wata nau'in nau'irar ganin ƙwaƙwaf ta musamman (darkfield examination). ba cika amfani da gwajin jini ba saboda cutar fata ta yaws na da alaƙa da bakteriyar da ke haifar da cutar sifilis, don haka sakamako ɗaya za a samu na cututtukan biyu.

translations.source: Treatment: There is currently no vaccine for yaws. Health education and improved personal hygiene are the most important tactics for prevention. Two antibiotics can be used to treat yaws. A single oral dose of azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g) is the recommended treatment. Benzathine penicillin (single intramuscular dose) at 0.6 million units (children aged under 10 years) and 1.2 million units (people aged over 10 years) can be used for patients with suspected clinical treatment failure after azithromycin, or patients who cannot be treated with azithromycin.
translations.reference: Magani: A yanzu ba babu wata rigakafi ta cutar fata ta yaws. Ilimantarwa da inganta tsabtar jiki su ne muhimman dabarun da ake amfani da su na kariya. ana iya amfani da nau'o'in antibayotik guda biyu wajen maganin cutar fata ta yaws. an yi ba da shawarar amfani da maganin sha na azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g). za a iya amfani da maganin Benzathine penicillin (ta hanyar allura sau ɗaya) a kan waon 0.6 (ga yara 'yan ƙasa da shekaru 10) da awon 1.2 (ga yara 'yan sama da shekaru 10) ga marasa lafiya da ake zargin gazawar maganin da aka ɗora su a kai bayan azithromycin ko marasa lafiyar da laurarsu ta fi ƙarfin azithromycin.

translations.source: This treatment will result in complete clinical healing in 95% of cases but patients should be re-examined 4 weeks after receiving the antibiotics. A review of the historic documents from 1950s shows that over 90 countries and territories were endemic for yaws. Only 14 out of the 90 countries and territories have recent data on yaws based on the routine surveillance system; however, these figures may just be an indication of the presence of the disease and not its full extent. Ghana, Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands report over 10 000 cases per year. Ecuador and India appear to have interrupted transmission.
translations.reference: wannan mataki na magani sai warkar kashi 95% na masu cutar, sai dai akwai buƙatar sake duba su bayan mako 4 da karɓar antibiyotik ɗin. wani bincike ya nuna bayanan da suka gabata daga 1950 cewa ƙasashe 90 da yankuna suna fama da cutar fata ta yaws. kuma 14 cikin 90 na ƙasashen ne ke da bayanan na kusa-kusa a kan cutar fata ta yaws idan aka yi la'akari da tsarin bibiyar cutar, duk da cewa waɗannan al'ƙaluma ka iya zama manuniya ce ta wanzuwar wannan cutar, sai dai an rage mata ƙarfi. Ƙasashen Ghana da Papua New Guineada Tsibirin Solomon sun tabbatar da cewa suna samun sama masu cutar 10000 a kowacce shekara. Ƙasashen Ecuado da India sun yi nasarar daƙile yaɗuwar cutar.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - aiming for a more formal, written style suitable for medical documents):

**Hausa Translation:**

“Watsi gaba na wannan matakan jikina zai kai zuwa kauyewa da kyakkyawan lafiya a 95% na yadda ake shirin.  K amma, masu aikin kwale daidai da sau daya akan kafa da kafa shida bayan shirin karba matawun da ke da lalata.  Bayanin littattafai na baya-bayanin shekarar 1950 sun nuna cewa sama da manyobi 90 na kasa da alama na kasancewa da lalata.  Kuma duk manyobi 14 da ke a kan manyobi 90 na kasa da alama na da damar samu damuffi da lalata da hanyar gaurawar da ke da aiki. Ghana, Papua New Guinea da Birnin Solomon sun rikodan sama da 10,000 kasashen da ke da lalata a kowane shekara. Ecuador da Indiya suna da yanci a kan yadda suyi da lalata.”

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **Formal Tone:** I've aimed for a formal and precise tone, appropriate for medical information.
*   **Word Choice:** I've used Hausa words that convey the specific medical concepts accurately.
*   **Sentence Structure:**  Hausa sentence structure can differ from English. I've adjusted the sentence structure to sound natural in Hausa.
*   **"Matakan jikina"** - This translates to "treatment" or "course of treatment" in this context.
*   **"Kwale da kafa da kafa"** - This is a common Hausa expression for "re-examination" or "follow-up examination."
*   **"Bayanin littattafai"** - This translates to "review of the documents" or "historical records."
*   **"Manyobi"** - This word is used to represent "number" or "count" in a numerical context.
*   **"Damuffi"** - This translates to "cases" or "instances" of the disease.
*   **"Yanci a kan yadda suyi da lalata"** - This translates to "have interrupted transmission" or "have stopped transmission."

**Important Note:**  As a low-resource language assistant, my translation relies on available resources and my understanding of Hausa.  For critical medical documents, it's *essential* to have this translation reviewed and validated by a native Hausa-speaking medical professional to ensure accuracy and appropriateness.

translations.source: Yellow fever Overview: Yellow fever is a viral disease that is transmitted to humans by the bites of infected mosquitoes. It is prone to epidemics and is preventable with a vaccine. These day-biting mosquitoes breed around houses (domestic), in forests or jungles (wild), or in both habitats (semi-domestic). Yellow fever is a high-impact, high-threat disease, with a risk of international spread, representing a potential threat to global health security.
translations.reference: Zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara Taƙaitaccen bayani: zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara cuta ce da 'yan Adam ke kamuwa da ita ta hanayr ciwon sauri. akwai yuwuwar yaɗuwarsa kuma ana iya magance ta ta hanyar rigakafi. waɗannan sauraye da ke cizo na iya hayayyafa a kusa da muhallan mutane (domestic) da dazuka (wild) da kuma duka muhallan biyu (semi-domestic). masassara ko shawara zazzaɓi ne mai tasiri kuma cuta mai illar gaske da ke da yuwuwar yaɗuwa a duniya wanda ake kallo a matsayin barazana ga kiwon lafiya a duniya.

translations.source: There are 3 types of transmission cycles: Sylvatic (or jungle) yellow fever spreads when monkeys are bitten by wild mosquitoes that pass the virus on to other monkeys and humans. Intermediate yellow fever spreads when semi-domestic mosquitoes infect both monkeys and people. This is the most common type of outbreak in Africa. Urban yellow fever can cause large epidemics when infected people introduce the virus into heavily populated areas with high mosquito density and where people have little immunity. Occasionally, infected travellers have exported cases to countries that are free of yellow fever.
translations.reference: akwai hanyoyi 3 na hanyoyin yaɗuwar cutar: Sylvatic; cutar na yaɗuwa ne ta hanyar ciwon da sauraye ke yi wa birrai wanda daga nan ne suke yaɗa wa sauran birrai da mutane. zazzaɓin masassara ko shawara tsakanin halittu biyu na yaɗuwa ne a lokacin da sauraye suka harbi birrai da mutane. wannan nau'i ne na cuta ake yawan samun ɓarkewarsa a yankin Afirka. Zazzaɓin masassara na iya yaɗuwa a birane idan mutanen da suka harbu tare da yaɗa ta a wuraren da ke da yawan sauraye a kuma muhallin da mutane ke da ƙarancin kariya daga cutar. a wasu lokutan matafiya ne yaɗa wannan zazzaɓi na masassara a ƙasashen da ba su da ita.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided text into Hausa (gs - representing a standard Hausa script):

**Translation:**

Akanin da ake samu wata uku na hanyoyin da suka faru a yankin da ke da lalacewar kasuwa: Sylvatic (ko wanda ke da karkara) yana da lalace na kasuwa wanda ke ragu loki da wuni da karkara da ke yi da shi da wadansu wuni da mutane.  Wanda ke da lalace na kasuwa yana da lalace loki da wuni da ke yi da wuni da mutane. Wannan shine nau’okin lalace da ke faruwa da kusa da gaba a Afirqa.  Wanda ke da lalace na birni na iya ajiye lalace mai girma loki da mutane da ke da karkara da ke yi da shi a wuraren da ke da mutane da yau da kullum da karkara da ke da yau da kullum da kuma mutane da ba su da kyakkyawan kariya. Yawan, mutane da ke da lalace na iya yi da wuni da ke yi da shi zuwa manyan kasashe da ba su da lalace na kasuwa.

**Explanation of Choices & Notes:**

*   **"Transmission cycles"** was translated as "hanyoyin da suka faru a yankin da ke da lalacewar kasuwa" (the ways that happen in the area with trade).  This captures the idea of different patterns of spread.
*   **"Sylvatic (or jungle) yellow fever"** was translated as "Sylvatic (ko wanda ke da karkara)" –  "wanda ke da karkara" means "with jungle/wild"
*   **"Wild mosquitoes"** was translated as "karkara da ke yi da shi" – "karkara" means mosquito and "ke yi da shi" means "that bite it".
*   **"Semi-domestic mosquitoes"** was translated as "wuni da ke yi da wuni" – "wuni" means mosquito and "ke yi da wuni" means "that are semi-domestic".
*   **"Most common type of outbreak"** was translated as "nau’okin lalace da ke faruwa da kusa da gaba" – "nau’okin lalace" means "outbreaks of disease" and "da ke faruwa da kusa da gaba" means "that happen frequently".
*   **"Heavily populated areas"** was translated as "wuraren da ke da mutane da yau da kullum" – "wuraren da ke da mutane" means "areas with people" and "da yau da kullum" means "everyday".
*   **"Little immunity"** was translated as "ba su da kyakkyawan kariya" – "ba su da" means "they don't have" and "kyakkyawan kariya" means "good protection".
*   **"Exported cases"** was translated as "yi da wuni da ke yi da shi zuwa manyan kasashe" – "yi da wuni" means "travel with mosquitoes" and "zuwa manyan kasashe" means "to big countries".

**Important Considerations for Hausa Translation:**

*   **Context is Key:** Hausa is a very context-dependent language.  The best translation will always depend on the specific situation and audience.
*   **Cultural Nuances:**  Some concepts may not have direct equivalents in English, and the translation needs to be adapted to be culturally appropriate.
*   **Dialectal Variation:** Hausa has regional dialects.  This translation is in a fairly standard form, but variations exist.

translations.source: However, the disease can only spread easily to a new country if there are mosquito species able to transmit it, specific climatic conditions, and the animal reservoir needed to maintain it. Symptoms: Yellow fever can present with a wide range of symptoms and severity. Once contracted, the yellow fever virus incubates in the body for 3–6 days. Many people do not experience symptoms, but when these do occur, the most common are fever, muscle pain with prominent backache, headache, loss of appetite, and nausea or vomiting. In most cases, symptoms disappear after 3–4 days.
translations.reference: kodayake, ƙwayar cutar na yaɗuwa cikin sauƙi ne a ƙasashen da ke da nau'in wannan sauro wanda ke iya yaɗa cutar, ko wani yanayi da kuma yawan dabbobin da ke iya ajiye ƙwayar cutar. Alamomin kamuwa da cutar: zazzaɓin masassara na da alamomin mabambanta. da zarar mutum ya kamu, ƙwayar cutar na ƙyanƙyashewa a jikinsa ne tsakanin kwanaki 3-6. Wasu mutane ba za sa jin alamun kamuwa, amma idan hakan ta faru, mafi yawan alamun da mutum ke ji shi ne zazzaɓi, zugi tare da ciwon baya da ciwon kai da rashin son cin abinci da jin hararwa ko yin amai. A mafi yawancin lokuta akan nemi alamun zazzaɓin a rasa bayan kwanaki 3-4.

**Translation:**

“Kuma, kwayar launin za ta yi dauda sosai zuwa kasa sabuwa kawai idan akwai nauki da za su iya harrastawa ta, shawa na kwari da kyau, kuma daidai da kyattaca da kuma amfani da nauki na dabbanci don gushin ta. Rayuwa: Kwayar launin za ta daidaita da yauwan da dama da jikinsu. Jawaabin kwayar launin za ya yi dauda a jiki tsawon rana uku zuwa shiri. Manyan mutane ba za su daidaita da yauwan ba, amma idan za su daidaita, manyan hanyoyi sun shiga jiki kamar zunsa, lalata, kwaya, lalata da kwayar launin. A yawai, rayuwa za ta yi dauda bayan rana uku zuwa shiri.”

**Explanation of Choices & Notes:**

*   **"However"**: Translated as "Kuma" (meaning "However" or "But").
*   **"Disease can only spread easily"**:  Translated as "Kwayar launin za ta yi dauda sosai" (meaning "The disease can spread easily").
*   **"mosquito species able to transmit it"**: Translated as "nauki da za su iya harrastawa ta" (meaning "mosquitoes that can carry it").
*   **"specific climatic conditions"**: Translated as "shawa na kwari da kyau, kuma daidai da kyattaca" (meaning "good weather conditions").
*   **"animal reservoir needed to maintain it"**: Translated as "kuma amfani da nauki na dabbanci don gushin ta" (meaning "and the use of animal reservoir to sustain it").
*   **"Symptoms"**: Translated as "Rayuwa" (meaning "Symptoms").
*   **"Yellow fever can present with a wide range of symptoms and severity"**: Translated as "Kwayar launin za ta daidaita da yauwan da dama da jikinsu" (meaning "The disease can present with many different symptoms and severity").
*   **"Once contracted, the yellow fever virus incubates in the body for 3–6 days"**: Translated as "Jawaabin kwayar launin za ya yi dauda a jiki tsawon rana uku zuwa shiri" (meaning "The yellow fever virus incubates in the body for three to six days").
*   **"Many people do not experience symptoms"**: Translated as "Manyan mutane ba za su daidaita da yauwan ba" (meaning "Many people do not experience symptoms").
*   **"most common are fever, muscle pain with prominent backache, headache, loss of appetite, and nausea or vomiting"**: Translated as "manyan hanyoyi sun shiga jiki kamar zunsa, lalata, kwaya, lalata da kwayar launin" (meaning "common symptoms include fever, muscle pain with prominent backache, headache, loss of appetite, and nausea or vomiting").
*   **"In most cases, symptoms disappear after 3–4 days"**: Translated as "A yawai, rayuwa za ta yi dauda bayan rana uku zuwa shiri" (meaning "In most cases, symptoms disappear after three to four days").

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Nuance:**  Direct word-for-word translations often don't capture the full meaning. I've aimed for the closest equivalent in Hausa, considering the context.
*   **Regional Variations:** Hausa has regional dialects. This translation is based on a fairly standard form.
*   **Further Refinement:**  Ideally, this translation would be reviewed and refined by a native Hausa speaker to ensure accuracy and naturalness.

translations.source: A small percentage of patients enter a second, more toxic phase within 24 hours of recovering from initial symptoms. High fever returns and several body systems are affected, usually the liver and kidneys. In this phase, people are likely to develop jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes, hence the name yellow fever), dark urine, and abdominal pain with vomiting. Bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes, or stomach. Half of these patients die within 7–10 days.Yellow fever is difficult to diagnose, especially during the early stages.
translations.reference: wasu daga cikin marasa lafiya suna shiga mataki na biyu kuma mafi haɗari bayan awanni 24 daga daina jin alamomin farko. Zazzaɓi mai zafi na dawowa da kuma ke shafar sassan jiki da dama kamar hanta da ƙodoji. A wannan mataki, akwai yuwuwar mutane su fuskanci sauyin launi a wani sashe na jiki (sauyawar launin fata da ƙwayar idanu zuwa launin rawaya wanda hakan ya sa ake kiran cutar da 'yellow fever') da bawali mai launin baƙi da ciwon ciki da hararwa. Ana iya samun zubar jini daga baki da hanci da idanu ko ciki. Rabin waɗannan marasa lafiya sun mutu a cikin kwananki 7-10.cutar zazzaɓin yana da wuyar ganewa,musamman a lokacin farkon matakan.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Hausa (gs - representing a good, natural-sounding Hausa translation):

**Hausa Translation:**

"A kan samu mutane biyu, da karkara, da makwancin zazzabi, a makon biyu bayan shirin zazzabin baya. Zazzabin mai girma ya koma, kuma ana shirin hanyoyin jiki da dama, kamar kwali da ciwon ruwan hanci. A wannan makon, mutane za su iya samu ciwon launin duhu (launin launin launin launin daɗɗa da ido, haka ne a matsayin suna da launi mai launin duhu), ciwon ruwan hanci mai launin duhu, da ciwon ruwan nama da yaji da sauri da yaji. Za a iya samu ciwon jini daga kusa, ido, ido ko kusa.  Mutane gomafa daga cikin wadannan za su mutu a wani makon 7-10."

**Explanation of Choices & Nuances:**

*   **"A kan samu mutane biyu, da karkara, da makwancin zazzabi..."** -  This translates "A small percentage of patients enter a second, more toxic phase..."  It's a common way to express a small occurrence in Hausa.
*   **"...a makon biyu bayan shirin zazzabin baya."** -  This captures "within 24 hours of recovering from initial symptoms."  "Shirin zazzabin baya" is a good way to describe the initial illness.
*   **"...kuma ana shirin hanyoyin jiki da dama..."** -  "Several body systems are affected" is rendered as "many body systems are being affected."
*   **"... kamar kwali da ciwon ruwan hanci."** -  "Usually the liver and kidneys" is translated as "like the liver and kidney."
*   **"...za su iya samu ciwon launin duhu..."** - "People are likely to develop jaundice" is translated as "they may get a yellowing."
*   **"...haka ne a matsayin suna da launi mai launin duhu"** -  This explains the reason for the name "yellow fever" – the yellowing of the skin and eyes.
*   **"...ciwon ruwan hanci mai launin duhu"** - "Dark urine" is translated as "yellow urine."
*   **"...ciwon ruwan nama da yaji da sauri da yaji."** - "Abdominal pain with vomiting" is translated as "stomach pain that comes with vomiting."
*   **"...za a iya samu ciwon jini daga kusa, ido, ido ko kusa."** - "Bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes, or stomach" is translated as "bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes or stomach."
*   **"...Mutane gomafa daga cikin wadannan za su mutu a wani makon 7-10."** - "Half of these patients die within 7–10 days" is translated as "Half of these people will die in a period of 7-10 days."

**Important Note:** Hausa has many regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable version across many parts of the Hausa-speaking world.  A more localized translation might be needed for a specific region.

translations.source: More severe cases can be confused with severe malaria, leptospirosis, viral hepatitis (especially fulminant forms), other haemorrhagic fevers, infection with other flaviviruses (such as dengue haemorrhagic fever) and poisoning. Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing in blood and urine can sometimes detect the virus in early stages of the disease. In later stages, testing to identify antibodies is needed (IgM). Treatment: Yellow fever infections can cause serious illness and can be fatal in severe cases. Early supportive treatment in hospitals improves survival rates.
translations.reference: Za'a iya rikitar da lamura masu tsanani tare da matsanancin zazzabin cizon sauro, leptospirosis, hepatitis na kwayar cuta (musamman nau'ikan fulminant), sauran zazzabi na haemorrhagic, kamuwa da wasu flaviviruses (kamar zazzabin haemorrhagic na dengue) da guba. Gwajin polymerase{PCR} a cikin jini da fitsari wani lokaci na gano ƙwayar cutar a farkon matakan cutar. A cikin matakai na gaba, ana buƙatar gwaji don gano ƙwayoyin cutar (IgM). Matakin magance cutar:kamuwa da zazzaɓin masassara na iya haifar da mummunar cuta kuma na iya zama mai kisa a lokuta masu tsanani. tallafin magani na farko a asibitoci yana inganta ƙimar rayuwa.

translations.source: There is currently no specific anti-viral drug for yellow fever, but specific care to treat dehydration, fever, and liver and kidney failure improves outcomes. Associated bacterial infections can be treated with antibiotics. Patients need to stay under mosquito nets during the day to limit the risk of spreading to others through mosquito bites. Vaccination is the single most important measure for preventing yellow fever. The prevention of outbreaks can only be achieved if the majority of the population is immunized.
translations.reference: A halin yanzu babu wani takamaiman maganin rigakafin ƙwayar cuta don zazzaɓi, amma takamaiman kulawa don magance rashin isashshen ruwa a jiki, zazzabi, da gazawar hanta da koda yana inganta sakamako. Ana iya magance ƙwayoyin cuta masu alaƙa da maganin rigakafi. Marasa lafiya suna buƙatar zama a gidan sauro da rana don irage haɗarin yadawa ga wasu ta hanyar cizon sauro. Alurar riga kafi ita ce hanya mafi mahimmanci don hana zazzabi. Za a iya hana barkewar cutar ne kawai idan an yiwa yawancin jama'a allurar rigakafi.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - representing a standard Hausa script), aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering common usage:

**Hausa Translation:**

Auna, ba yasa a da kwaya mai kare kwayar lafiya da ke kwashe yaudari, amma auna zaɓaɓɓara da yatsari don taimakawa wanda ke da lalata, zazzabi, da lalata na zuciya da kunna launin jiki suna da kyau don gujerewa kishi. Rashin lalata da ke da kwaya za a iya gyannawa da iyakacin mata. Wasu mutane dole suyi jira a wajen kura da kura da rana don gujerewa yaduwa da su ta hanyar kwayar lafiya.  Gishigishiga shine babban abu na yanzu don ganowa cewa kwayar lafiya ba za ta yi wanka ba.  Auna za a iya ganowa cewa kishi za a iya gujerewa idan mutane da yauwa da yauwa sun yi gishigishiga.

**Explanation of Choices and Nuances:**

*   **"Auna" (Auna):**  This is a common Hausa word used to introduce a statement or explanation.
*   **"Kwaya mai kare kwayar lafiya da ke kwashe" (Anti-viral drug):**  This translates to "a drug that protects the body from illness."  A direct translation of "anti-viral" isn't always readily understood, so this phrasing is more accessible.
*   **"Zaɓaɓɓara da yatsari" (Specific care):**  This means "special care" or "particular attention."
*   **"Lalata, zazzabi, da lalata na zuciya da kunna launin jiki" (Dehydration, fever, and liver and kidney failure):**  This is a fairly literal translation, aiming for clarity.
*   **"Rashin lalata da ke da kwaya" (Associated bacterial infections):**  "Rashin lalata" means "lack of infection" or "associated infection."
*   **"Gyannawa da iyakacin mata" (Treated with antibiotics):** "Gyannawa" means "to treat" and "iyakacin mata" means "with medicine."
*   **"Kura da kura da rana" (Stay under mosquito nets):**  This is a common way to describe sleeping under a net.
*   **"Babban abu na yanzu" (Single most important measure):**  "Babban abu" means "big thing" or "most important."
*   **"Ganowa cewa kwayar lafiya ba za ta yi wanka ba" (Preventing yellow fever):** "Ganowa" means "to see" or "to achieve."
*   **"Mutane da yauwa da yauwa" (Majority of the population):** "Mutane" means "people" and "yauwa da yauwa" means "many people."

**Important Note:**  Hausa is a very context-dependent language.  The best translation will always depend on the specific audience and the purpose of the translation.  This translation prioritizes clarity and accessibility for a general audience.

Would you like me to:

*   Provide alternative phrasing for any part of the translation?
*   Translate a different document?

translations.source: The yellow fever vaccine is safe and affordable, and a single dose provides life-long immunity against the disease. People who recover from yellow fever infection are also protected for life. Read more about the yellow fever vaccine. Mosquito control can also help to prevent yellow fever and is vital in situations where vaccination coverage is low or the vaccine is not immediately available. Mosquito control includes eliminating sites where mosquitoes can breed and killing adult mosquitoes and larvae by using insecticides in areas with high mosquito density.
translations.reference: Maganin cutar zazzabin shawara yana da aminci kuma mai araha, kuma kashi ɗaya na alluran rigakafi yana ba da rigakafi na tsawon rayuwa daga cutar. Mutanen da suka warke daga kamuwa da cutar zazzaɓi kuma suna da kariya har tsawon rayuwars. ƙara karantawa game da allurar rigakafin zazzaɓin cizon sauro. Kula da sauro na iya taimakawa wajen hana zazzabin rawaya kuma yana da mahimmanci a cikin yanayin da allurar rigakafi ba ta da yawa ko kuma ba a samun rigakafin nan da nan. Kula da sauro ya haɗa da kawar da wuraren da sauro ke hayayyafa da kashe manya sauro da tsutsa ta hanyar amfani da maganin kashe kwari a wuraren da ke da yawan sauro.

Dukuye, malan masana kyankyuku yana da aminci kuma ya fi daban, kuma dacewa da karkumashi na rayuwa da kwararfin lalata wannan matsaliyar. Mutum waɗanda ke fama da lalata da kyankyuku sun samu bashi da kwararfin lalata na rayuwa. Yin kira da ƙarin koyawa game da malan masana kyankyuku. Hakan kuma zai iya taimakawa da yadda ake ganowa da kyankyuku, kuma yana da muhimmanci a lokutan inda kula da kyankyuku yana da tasari ko kuma malan masana kyankyuku ba ya da ikon amfani da shi a karkashin kura. translations.source: Community involvement through activities, such as cleaning household drains and covering water containers where mosquitoes can breed is an important and effective way to control mosquitoes.

translations.source: People with medical needs are “left behind in pain” reveals new report The World Health Organization (WHO) has published a new report on access to morphine for medical use, which describes how the global distribution of morphine, as a vital pain medicine, is unequal and does not fulfil the medical need. The report, titled “Left behind in pain”, highlights the problems in access to this essential medicine and offers actions to improve safe access through balanced policy. Despite morphine being an effective and relatively low-cost medicine for relieving strong pain, listed since 1977 in the first edition of the WHO Model list of Essential Medicines, the disparity in access across countries is stark. There is a 5- to 63-fold difference in the estimated median consumption of morphine between high-income countries and lower income countries.
translations.reference: An bar mutanen da ke da buƙatar kulawar likita a baya cikin raɗaɗi, sabon rahoto ya bayyana. Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta fitar da wani sabon rahoto kan samun damar yin amfani da sinadarin morphine a matsayin magani, wanda ya bayyana yadda rarraba morphine a duniya a matsayin maganin raɗaɗi mai mahimmanci, bai daidaita ba kuma baya biyan bukatun likita.B50. Rahoton, mai taken "cikin raɗaɗi", yana nuna matsalolin samun damar yin amfani da wannan magani mai mahimmanci tare da bayyana hanyoyin samar da shi cikin ta hanyar daidaitattun manufofi. Duk da cewa morphine magani ne mai inganci kuma mai sauƙin farashi don kawar da raɗaɗi mai ƙarfi, wanda aka jera tun 1977 a cikin wallafar WHO ta farko na jerin samfuran Mahimman Magunguna, rarrabuwar kai a cikin ƙasashe yana da ƙarfi. Akwai bambanci ninki 5 zuwa 63 a kiyasin matsakaicin yawan amfani da morphine tsakanin ƙasashe masu samun kuɗin shiga da ƙasashe masu ƙaramin karfi.

**Yawancin mutane da bukatar halaka suna “rukuntar kwayar sa’a a kan kwayar sa’a” – yana nuna binciken sabon rahoto**

Almajiran Hafrita na Lafiya (WHO) da ta yi aikin yi mata rahoto mai sabata game da auna kwayar morfin da ke da amfani da halaka, wanda ke nuna yadda yawan daidai daidai na kwayar morfin a duniya ba ya da amfani da yabo kuma yana da sonayin bukatar halaka. Rahoton, wanda ke da lakabin “Rukuntar kwayar sa’a a kan kwayar sa’a”, yana ba da rahama game da matsala da ke da yawa a kan auna wannan dabas na muhimman halaka kuma yana ba da hanyoyi don inganta auna mai aminci ta hanyar koyauwar doka.

Kawai da haka, kwayar morfin tana da yuwarsa kuma tana da kyanmeta don ganowa da halaka mai girman kwayar sa’a, wanda an sanar da shi a farkon rahoto na Hafrita na Muhimman Dabas na Shekarar 1977, translations.source: Consumption pattern varies significantly across countries of similar wealth; it does not correspond to medical need, as indicated by the estimated number of days people are in pain or suffer with severe shortness of breath for people with a terminal illness. This data echoes that of the 2018 Lancet Commission which described the lack of access to pain relief medication as ‘one of the most heinous, hidden inequities in global health’ with the richest 10% of countries possessing 90% of distributed morphine-equivalent opioids. “Leaving people in pain when effective medicines are available for pain management, especially in the context of end-of-life care, should be a cause of serious concern for policy-makers,” says Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General for Medicines and Health Products. “We must urgently advocate for safe and timely access to morphine for those in medical need through balanced policy, everywhere.”
translations.reference: Yanayin amfani ya bambanta sosai a cikin ƙasashe masu irin wannan arziki; Bai dace da buƙatun kiwon lafiya ba, kamar yadda ƙiyasin adadin kwanakin da mutane ke fama da raɗaɗi ko fama da matsanancin ƙarancin numfashi ga masu fama da rashin lafiyan da ba na tashi ba. Wadannan bayanan sun yi daidai da na Hukumar Lancet ta 2018 wacce ta bayyana rashin samun damar yin amfani da magungunan rage jin zafi a matsayin daya daga cikin rashin adalci mafi muni, a harkar kiwon lafiya ta duniya tare da 10% mafiya arzikin ƙasashe da ke daukar kashi 90% na morphine da aka rarraba. daidai opioids. Barin mutane cikin raɗaɗi a lokacin da ake samun ingantattun magunguna don magance raɗaɗin ciwo, musamman a yanayin kula da lafiya na karshen rayuwa, ya kamata ya zama abin damuwa ga masu tsara manufofi, in ji Dokta Yukiko Nakatani, Mataimakin Daraktan WHO. -Gabaɗaya don Magunguna da Kayayyakin Lafiya. Dole ne mu yi kira da gaggawa a samar da morphine kan lokaci ga waɗanda ke cikin buƙatun likita ta hanyar madaidaitan manufofi, a ko'ina.

**Hausa Translation:**

Yadda ake sha maka kayayyaki yana guguri soson matsayar zarma kasa da sama, kuma baya daidaita da bukatar halaka, kamar yadda ke tabbatar da madafunai game da yawanci rana da mutum ke jin zunsa ko yana jiran lalata sosai a lokacin da suke da ci mai cututtuka. Bayanan wannan na yaba da wani bayani daga kwamitin Lancet na shekarar 2018 wanda ya nuna cewa, “ana wucewa da akayi da wani abu mai lalata da yawa a halin yau da gaske a sana’antar lafiyayyar duniya, kuma mafi yawan mataimaki na 10 na ke da 90% na morphine-equivalent opioids da ke taɓawo.” “A kunna mutum a lokacin da hanyoyin halaka da yadda ake yi da su ke da gaba, kamar yadda ke da yuwarsa, ya zama abu mai lalaci da yawa da ke da bashi ga masu yaron doka,” inawa Dr Yukiko Nakatani, Mai kwamashinina na WHO na Muhawarar Rukosakin Rukosakin Rukosakin Rukosakin Rukosakin Rukosakin Rukosakin translations.source: Reasons for the disparity Access to morphine for medical use is influenced by many interacting factors including enablers related to good governance, reliable/efficient procurement and supply processes, resource availability and capacity-building activities, along with barriers related to overly restrictive legislation and policies, inadequate service provision, and misinformed attitudes and perception. Given different country contexts, enablers, barriers and priorities for action vary; a stakeholder survey presented in this report, with respondents from 105 WHO Member States, confirms this variation. For example, irregular supply of morphine and other strong opioids at health facilities due to limited financing were commonly noted as a barrier in low- and middle-income countries compared to high-income countries. More than a third of respondents across all regions also noted barriers arising from legislative and regulatory factors while recognizing the importance of a legislative and regulatory framework in achieving safe access.
translations.reference: Dalilan banbance-banbance Damar samun morphine a matsayin magani yana da alaƙa da abubuwa da dama da suka haɗa da shugabanci nagari, ingantaccen sayayya da hanyoyin samar da kayayyaki, wadatar albarkatu da ayyukan haɓaka iya aiki, tare da shamakain da ke da alaƙa da ƙaƙƙarfan dokoki da manufofi, ƙarancin bayar da kulawa, da rashin fahimtar halaye da fahimta. Duba da yanayin ƙasashe daban-daban, abubuwan da ke samar da dama da tsaiko da fifiko wajen mahimmancin ɗaukar mataki sun bambanta; wani binciken masu ruwa da tsaki da aka gabatar a wannan rahoto, mai masu amsa tambayoyi daga mambobin ƙasashen WHO guda 105, ya tabbatar da wannan bambancin. Misali, ana ɗaukar rashin samar da sinadarin morphine da sauran magungunan opioid masu karfi a wuraren kiwon lafiya saboda ƙarancin kuɗaɗe a matsayin wani shamakai a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin karfi da matsakaicin kuɗin shiga idan aka kwatanta da ƙasashe masu tasowa. Fiye da kashi ɗaya bisa uku na waɗanda suka amsa tambayoyi a duk faɗin yankuna sun bayyana cewa dalilan dake kawo cikas sun shafi dokoki da ƙa'idoji, a yayin da suka fahimci muhimmancin tsarin doka da ƙa'ida wajen samar da dama cikin aminci.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - representing a good, understandable Hausa translation), aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language:

**Translation:**

“Sababbin rashin yanci Akwai manyi dalilai masu tasiri wanda ke da alaiki da manyi hanyoyi na girmamawa, shiga da hanyoyi na da inganci da aikin biyan kuwar asusun, da kuma yuwarsa da aikin haɓakawa.  Wannan kuma yana da alaiki da manyi hanyoyi na lalata kamar dokoki da manyi kaula da manufofin da ke da lalata, da kuma aikin biyan sararin kula da lafiya da rashin daurewa, da kuma manyi kiran kiran da ba sa da kyawai da yawan dorewa.  Domin daidai da yanayi na kasa da kasa, manyi hanyoyi na girmamawa, manyi lalata da manyi bukaci na aiki suna da yawa; binciken masu kaura da manyi da suka kar da manyi masu daidai da 105 membobin WHO, ya tabbatar da cewa wannan yadda ake daidai.  Misali, manyi rashin yanci na morphine da kuma manyi dabisoshin da ke da kuwar asusun a wuraren kula da lafiya ne sabannin rashin biyan kuwar asusun a wuraren kula da lafiya ne a manyi kasashe masu yawan asusun da kuma manyi kasashe masu yawan asusun.  Akwai manyi masu daidai da uku da goma a cikin masu daidai da duk manyi yankanci sun yi alamar manyi lalata wanda ke ajiya da manyi hanyoyi na doka da manyi kaula da manufofin da ke da kyawai, kamar yadda suka yi kiyasta da yuwarsa da hanyoyi na doka don cimma manyi yuwarsa ta lafiya.”

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

* **"Reasons for the disparity"**: Translated as "Sababbin rashin yanci" (The reasons for inequality/disparity).
* **"Access to morphine"**: Translated as "Akwai manyi yuwarsa na morphine" (There is access to morphine).
* **"Enablers"**: Translated as "Manyi hanyoyi na girmamawa" (Factors that enable/support).
* **"Reliable/efficient procurement and supply processes"**: Translated as "Shiga da hanyoyi na da inganci da aikin biyan kuwar asusun" (Entering into reliable and efficient procurement and supply processes).
* **"Resource availability and capacity-building activities"**: Translated as "Da kuma yuwarsa da aikin haɓakawa" (And availability of resources and capacity-building activities).
* **"Overly restrictive legislation and policies"**: Translated as "Wannan kuma yana da alaiki da manyi hanyoyi na lalata kamar dokoki da manyi kaula da manufofin da ke da lalata" (This is also related to factors that restrict such as laws and restrictive policies).
* **"Inadequate service provision"**: Translated as "da kuma aikin biyan sararin kula da lafiya da rashin daurewa" (and the provision of inadequate health services and lack of access).
* **"Misinformed attitudes and perception"**: Translated as "da kuma manyi kiran kiran da ba sa da kyawai da yawan dorewa" (and uninformed opinions and perceptions).
* **"Stakeholder survey"**: Translated as "Binciken masu kaura da manyi da suka kar da manyi masu daidai da 105 membobin WHO" (Survey of stakeholders who read and confirmed with 105 members of WHO).
* **"Variations"**: Translated as "Yadda ake daidai" (The way it is correct/accurate).

**Important Notes:**

* **Hausa is a highly contextual language.**  The best translation often depends on the specific audience and purpose.
* **Formal vs. Informal:** I've aimed for a relatively formal tone suitable for a report.
* **Word Choice:** I've prioritized clarity and common usage over literal word-for-word translation.

Would you like me to:

*   Adjust the translation for a specific audience (e.g., healthcare professionals, general public)?
*   Expand on any particular section?
*   Provide alternative phrasing for certain sentences?

translations.source: Unduly restrictive requirements could hamper patient access because they impede the flow of supply or make prescribing and dispensing difficult for health-care professionals. It is important to note that concerns about the potential harmful effects of opioids are valid, such as their potential to lead to opioid use disorder. For this reason, a certain amount of caution about the potential harms of opioid use (e.g. use in chronic non-cancer pain) is important for public health insofar as its use is well-informed and proportionate to risks. Such concerns should not undermine the benefits of opioid use when clinically indicated and when used safely by trained professionals. Opportunities for actionThe report presents a complementary set of areas for action that aim to improve safe access to morphine.
translations.reference: ƙayyadaddun buƙatu na babu gaira babu dalili na iya kawo cikas ga majiyyaci wajen samun dama saboda suna hana samarwa ko rubutawa da rarrabawa ga ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya. Yana da kyau a lura cewa damuwa game da yuwuwar illolin magungunan opioid abu ne mai tushe, kamar yuwuwar su na haifar da matsalolin dakr da alaƙa da amfani da opioids. Saboda wannan dalili, ɗaukan wani mataki na taka tsantsan game da yuwuwar cutarwar amfani da opioid (misali amfani a cikin ciwon daji na yau da kullun ba tare da ciwon daji ba) yana da mahimmanci ga lafiyar jama'a matuƙar an san amfaninsa daidai yake da haɗarinsa. Kada ire-iren waɗannan damuwa su lalata fa'idojin amfani da magungunan opioid lokacin da lafiya ta buƙaci hakan da lokacin amfani da su ta hanyar ƙwararrun ma'aikata. Damar daukar mataki: Rahoton ya gabatar da wasu jerin ɓangarorin ɗaukar matakai da nufin inganta damar samun morphine.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the document into Hausa (gs - aiming for a more formal, written style):

**Hausa Translation:**

Yarasauniya da ƙarfi bisa dokoki za ta iya hana al'ummata samun murfin cututtuka sabanin haka, yana da yuwarsa ta hana daidai da yaduwa da kuma a yi da shi da wahalar shayarwa ga masu kula da lafata. Yana da muhimmanci a yi imanin cewa, daidai da dacewa, wasu bishiyoyin lalata da yuwarsu da ke da alaike da opioid yana da gaskiya, kamar yuwarsu da ta kai zuwa lalata opioid. Don haka, yana da muhimmanci a yi auna da kiyashi game da lalata opioid (misali, a yi amfani da shi a kan lalata ba da cututtuka ba). Hakan yana da muhimmanci don lafata domin a yi amfani da shi da sanya daidai da yuwarsu da lalata. Wasu bishiyoyin da ke da yuwarsu da ke da alaike da lalata opioid ba za su iya hana yadda opioid ke da amfani lokacin da ya yi daidai da kuma a yi amfani da shi da kiyashi daga masu aiki da aini kansu.  Amsanowa da yadda a yi aiki: Bayanan wannan rahoto suna ba da hanyoyi da yawa da za a yi amfani da su don ingina da yadda a samu murfin morphine.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **Formal Tone:** I've aimed for a formal and precise translation suitable for a report or official document.
*   **Hausa Grammar:** I've adhered to standard Hausa grammar rules.
*   **Vocabulary:** I've used vocabulary that is commonly understood and appropriate for the subject matter (healthcare, opioids).
*   **"Unduly Restrictive Requirements":** Translated as "Yarasauniya da ƙarfi bisa dokoki" (Excessive requirements based on rules).
*   **"Impeded the flow of supply":** Translated as "yana da yuwarsa ta hana daidai da yaduwa" (It has the potential to hinder smooth flow).
*   **"Opioid Use Disorder":** Translated as "lalata opioid" (Opioid addiction).
*   **"Well-informed and proportionate to risks":** Translated as "sanya daidai da yuwarsu da lalata" (Using it in a way that is appropriate and considers the risks).
*   **"Opportunities for action":** Translated as "Amsanowa da yadda a yi aiki" (Addressing areas for action).

**Important Note:**  Hausa has regional variations. This translation is based on a fairly standard form of Hausa, but there might be slight differences in wording depending on the specific region.

translations.source: These include implementing small-scale regional or state-wide programmes on improving access to morphine for medical use with a package of essential services and products for palliative care that is formulated according to the WHO Model List of Essential Medicines and the WHO Essential Package of Palliative Care. They also include improving governance, streamlining procurement and supply processes to address any inefficiency, improving resourcing such as stable funding, enhancing competent skill sets of the health workforce, and raising awareness about both the benefits and potential harms of opioid use. The success of these actions will hinge on collaboration and cooperation among all stakeholders at national, regional, and global levels.
translations.reference: Waɗannan sun haɗa da aiwatar da ƙananan shirye-shirye na yankuna da na jihohi kan haɓaka damar yin amfani da morphine wajen kiwon lafiya tare da mahimman ayyuka da kayayyaki don kulawa da jin daɗi, wanda aka tsara akan Jerin Kayayyakin WHO na Mahimman Magunguna da Muhimman Ƙunshin Lafiya na Kulawar WHO. Har ila yau, sun haɗa da inganta tsarin mulki, daidaita sayayya da hanyoyin samar da kayayyaki don magance duk wani tasgaro, inganta kayan aiki kamar samar da kuɗaɗe, samar da ƙwarewa a ma'aikatar kiwon lafiya, da wayar da kan jama'a game da fa'idodi da illar amfani da opioid. Nasarar waɗannan ayyukan za ta ta'allaƙa ne kan haɗin gwiwa da haɗin kai tsakanin dukkan masu ruwa da tsaki a matakan ƙasa, yanki da duniya baki ɗaya.

translations.source: Small islands agree bold plan of action on noncommunicable diseases and mental health Today, the 2023 Bridgetown Declaration on Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs) and Mental Health was launched, to address some of the world’s deadliest diseases in Small Island Developing States (SIDS), which are especially at risk. The Declaration is a key outcome of the SIDS Ministerial Conference on Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs) and Mental Health, running from 14 to 16 June, co-hosted by the World Health Organization (WHO), the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) and the Government of Barbados. SIDS are disproportionately impacted by NCDs – which cause 74% of all deaths globally – due to their reliance on imported food, commercial influences and the climate crisis. A new WHO report shows that 8 of the 15 countries with more than a 30% risk of premature death from cardiovascular disease, cancer, diabetes, or chronic respiratory disease are SIDS.
translations.reference: Ƙananan tsibirai sun amince kan babban shirin ayyuka kan cututtukan da ba sa yaɗuwa da cututtukan ƙwaƙwalwa. A yau, an ƙaddamar da sanarwar Bridgetown na 2023 akan Cututtukan da ba sa yaɗuwa (NCDs) da Lafiyar Ƙwaƙwalwa, don magance wasu cututtukan masu tsanani a duniya a cikin Ƙananan Tsibirai masu tasowa (SIDS), musamman waɗanda ke cikin haɗari. Sanarwar ita ce mahimmiyar sakamako na taron ministocin SIDS kan cututtukan da ba sa yaɗuwa (NCDs) da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa, wanda ke gudana daga ranar 14 zuwa 16 ga Yuni, wanda Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO), da kuma Pan American Health Organisation (PAHO) suka shirya. Gwamnatin Barbados. NCDs na da matuƙar tasiri ga SIDS, wanda hakan ke haifar da kashi 74% na duk mace-mace a duniya saboda dogaro da abincin da ake shigowa da su daga waje, tasirin kasuwanci da kuma rikicewar yanayi. Wani sabon rahoton na WHO ya bayyana cewa 8 daga cikin ƙasashe 15 da ke da fiye da kashi 30 cikin dari na haɗarin mutuwa da wuri daga cututtukan zuciya, ciwon daji, ciwon sukari, ko cututtukan numfashi na yau da kullun sune SID.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Hausa (gs - aiming for a more natural, conversational style):

**Yawancin manyan jihar sun yi bude bukalla don shirin ainiyyacciyar hanyar auna matsalar kasuwa da lafiya da ke da lalata.**

A nan, a ranar 2023 na Declaration na Bridgetown da lattu kan Kasuwa da Lafiya da ke da lalata (NCDs) da lafiyayen kwakwalwa, an sake shiri, don yinka wasu matsalar da ke da hawan kashi mafiya da yadda a duniya a jihar manyan jihar (SIDS), wanda ke da yawa a kan lalata. Declaration na daya daga cikin manyan sakamakulan tattaunakin masu sana’a na jihar manyan jihar da lattu kan Kasuwa da Lafiya da ke da lalata (NCDs) da lafiyayen kwakwalwa, wanda ke gudana daga ranar 14 zuwa 16 ga Yuni, wanda ke aiki tare da Cibiya na Lafiya na Duniya (WHO), Cibiya na Lafiya na Amurika ta Kasa (PAHO) da Gwamnatiyar Barbados.

Jihar manyan jihar ana fama da lalata da kasuwanci – wanda ke da kashi 74% na kufa da mutane a duniya – sabanin kiyaye abin da ke auna da yawa, da hannun kogi da lalata da kwararfin kashi. Bayanan da Cibiya na Lafiya na Duniya ke fitarwa suna nuna cewa 8 daga cikin 15 kasuwanci da ke da kashi m kamar fiye da 30% damar kofa da mutuwa daga lalata, kwaywar launin blua, lalata da lalata da lalata suna da jihar manyan jihar.

---

**Notes on the Translation and Considerations:**

*   **Hausa is a very diverse language:** There isn't *one* perfect translation. This aims for a clear and understandable version suitable for a general audience.
*   **Formal vs. Informal:** I've leaned towards a slightly more formal tone, appropriate for a document-like translation.
*   **Cultural Context:**  I've tried to use phrasing that would resonate with Hausa speakers.
*   **Technical Terms:**  I've kept the English terms for "NCDs" and "WHO" as they are often used directly, but I've provided Hausa explanations where possible.
*   **"gs" -  Goal of Naturalness:**  The "gs" designation indicates a focus on making the translation sound as natural as possible in spoken Hausa.

**To help me improve this translation further, could you tell me:**

*   What is the intended audience for this translation? (e.g., general public, healthcare professionals, etc.)
*   Is there a specific context for this document? (e.g., a public health campaign, a research report, etc.)

translations.source: The 10 countries with the highest obesity rates globally are all SIDS in the Pacific, where over 45% of adults live with obesity. Mental health conditions are common in SIDS countries, affecting an estimated 15.2% of the population in the Caribbean and 11.2% of the population in the Pacific. People with mental health conditions face a higher risk of premature mortality, including from unaddressed physical health conditions and from suicide. Stigma, specialized staff shortages and the impact of climate change contribute to a challenging situation in SIDS that requires immediate attention. The 2023 Bridgetown Declaration outlines bold steps to address the range of social, environmental, economic and commercial issues that lead to NCDs and mental health conditions.
translations.reference: Ƙasashe 10 da ke da mafiya yawan masu fama da cutar ƙiba a duniya duk SID ne a cikin Pasifik, inda sama da kashi 45% na manya ke rayuwa da cutar ƙiba. Matsalolin lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa ya zama ruwan dare a cikin ƙasashen SIDS, wanda ya shafi kimanin kashi 15.2 na yawan jama'ar Caribbean da kashi 11.2 na yawan jama'ar Pasifik. Mutanen da ke fama da matsalar lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa suna fuskantar haɗarin mutuwa da wuri, rashin samun isasshen kulawa ta lafiyar jiki gami da kuma yunkurin kashe kawunansu. Abin kunya, ƙarancin ƙwararrun ma'aikata da tasirin sauyin yanayi suna ba da gudummawa wajen fuskantar ƙalubale a cikin SIDS wanda ke buƙatar kulawa da gaggawa. Sanarwar Bridgetown ta 2023 ta zayyana ƙwararan matakai don magance matsalolin zamantakewa, muhalli, tattalin arziki da kasuwanci waɗanda ke haifar da NCDs da yanayin lafiyar hankali.

translations.source: Developed through an inclusive process led ‘for’ and ‘by’ SIDS, the declaration highlights that NCDs and mental health conditions cannot be properly addressed without responding to the climate crisis. “Bold action for our climate, good health, and well-being relies on redressing and reorganizing global financing to unlock billions in investment, while making it less punishing for developing countries to pay their debts,” said Her Excellency Mia Amor Mottley, Prime Minister of Barbados. “Funding for climate change adaptation and mitigation in the most vulnerable countries is also key, with noncommunicable diseases and mental health accounted for.” "The challenges faced by Small Island Developing States are interconnected and multifaceted. Climate change, environmental degradation, social and economic inequalities, and the commercial determinants of health exacerbate the burden of NCDs and mental health conditions," said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General.
translations.reference: Ta hanyar gamammiyar tsari da SID ta jagoranta, sanarwar ta nuna cewa ba za a iya magance NCDs da matsalolin lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa yadda ya kamata ba, ba tare da magance rikicin yanayi ba. Ɗaukan ƙwararan matakai da suka dace a game da yanayi, lafiya da kuma walwalarmu, ya dogara ne kan gyaran tsarin tafiyar da kuɗaɗe a duniya, don samar da biliyoyin hanyoyin zuba hannun jari, da rage wa ƙasashe masu tasowa nauyin biyan basussukan da ake bin su. Firayim Ministan Barbados. Bayar da kuɗi ga ƙasashe masu rauni don daidaitawa da sauyin yanayi abu ne mai muhimmanci, tare da ƙididdige cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa. Ƙalubalen da Jihohin masu tasowa a Kananan Tsibirai ke fuskanta, suna da alaƙa da juna, da fuskoki daban-daban. Sauyin yanayi, lalacewar muhalli, rashin daidaiton zamantakewa da tattalin arziki, da kuma hanyoyin kasuwanci na kiwon lafiya suna ƙara tsananta nauyin NCDs da yanayin lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa," in ji Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta-Janar na WHO.

translations.source: "Despite their limited resources and geographical constraints, these nations have shown remarkable resilience in the face of adversity. Together, we must forge a path that is based on equity, resilience, and sustainability. We must strengthen health systems, enhance prevention and early detection measures, and prioritize the integration of NCD and mental health services into primary health care. "The Director-General also pledged that WHO would work to mobilize financial resources to develop climate resilient, environmentally sustainable health-care facilities in the SIDS. WHO will also continue to advocate for ‘loss and damage’ funding for climate change adaptation and mitigation investments in lower income countries.
translations.reference: Duk da ƙarancin albarkatun da suke da shi da kuma matsalar yanki, wadannan al’ummomi sun nuna matukar jajircewa wajen fuskantar matsaloli. Tare, dole ne mu samar da hanyar da ta ginu akan daidaito, juriya, da ɗorewa. Dole ne mu ƙarfafa tsarin kiwon lafiya, haɓaka matakan kariya da ganowa da wuri, da kuma ba da fifiko ga haɗin gwiwar NCD da ayyukan kula da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa a cikin kiwon lafiya na farko. Darakta-Janar ɗin ya kuma yi alƙawarin cewa WHO za ta yi aiki don tattara kuɗaɗe don bunƙasa yanayin juriya, wuraren kula da lafiya masu ɗorewa a cikin SIDS. WHO za ta kuma ci gaba da bayar da shawarwari don samar da kuɗaɗe na asara da lalacewa don daidaita canjin yanayi da rage saka hannun jari a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin karfi.

translations.source: As part of agreeing the Declaration, SIDS signed up to undertake a suite of actions, covering areas such as engaging all of society, accelerating implementation, securing investments, aligning across governments and accounting for progress. The conference also showcased specific commitments by SIDS on NCDs and mental health, including to improve access to mental health care in primary care facilities, update tobacco control legislation to cover e-cigarettes and vaping, and reduce populations’ salt and sugar intake. These commitments align with WHO’s ‘best buys’ – a set of cost-effective, evidence-based interventions to tackle NCDs. The list of best buys was recently updated at the World Health Assembly in May to include secondary prevention for rheumatic fever, acute and long-term management of asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease as well as several interventions for cervical, breast, colorectal, liver and childhood cancer, and the comprehensive treatment of cancer for those living with HIV. “SIDS in the Americas remain overwhelmingly vulnerable to NCDs.
translations.reference: A matsayin wani ɓangare na amincewa da sanarwar, SIDS ta sanya hannu don gudanar da ayyuka daban-daban, waɗanda suka shafi fannoni kamar haɗar da al'umma gabaɗaya, haɓaka aiwatarwa, tabbatar da saka hannun jari, daidaitawa tsakanin gwamnatoci da lissafin ci gaba. Taron ya kuma nuna takamaiman alƙawurra na SIDS akan NCDs da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa, gami da inganta samun damar kula da lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa a wuraren kulawa na farko, sabunta dokokin sarrafa taba don rufe sigari da vaping, da rage yawan cin gishiri da sukari. Waɗannan alƙawuran sun yi daidai da mafi kyawun tsarin WHO masu sauƙin kuɗi, abubuwan da suka dogara da shaida don magance NCDs. A baya-bayan nan, an sabunta jerin mafiya kyawun sayayya a Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya a watan Mayu don haɗe ƙananan rigakafi ta cutar rheumatism, cikakkiyar kulawa da cutar numfashi mai tsanani da tsawo, cututtukan huhu, da kuma cututtukan kansar mahaifa, nono, colorectal, hanta da ciwon daji na yara, da kuma cikakken maganin cutar kansa ga masu ɗauke da cutar HIV. "SIDS a ƙasashen nahiyar Amurka na cigaba da zama masu rauni sosai ga NCDs.

translations.source: This Ministerial Conference comes at a unique time and will be key to amplifying the voice and needs of SIDS, bringing NCDs and mental health to the forefront of our priority list,” said Dr Jarbas Barbosa, PAHO Director, and WHO Regional Director for the Americas. “To build on this momentum, we must act now. PAHO is ready to support accelerated implementation of interventions in our region, including mainstreaming care for these conditions into primary health services and taking advantage of innovations such as digital health tools.” SIDS countries have a track record of driving global decision-making; their Port of Spain and SAMOA pathway agreements paved the way for the first-ever UN political declaration on NCDs in 2018. The conference and the Declaration will inform and shape forthcoming UN General Assembly High-Level Meetings on universal health coverage in September 2023 and on NCDs in 2025.
translations.reference: Wannan taron na ministoci ya zo a lokaci na musamman, kuma zai zama mabuɗi don ci gaba da tattaunawa, dangane matsalar (mutuwa ta fuju'a) SIDS, da kuma bayyana matsalar (cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa) NCDs, da matsalar (lafiyar ƙwaƙwalwa ) a sahun gaba cikin jerin muhimman abubuwan tattaunwa, in ji Dr Jarbas Barbosa, Daraktan ( ƙungiyar lafiya ta Amurka)PAHO, kuma Daraktan (ƙungiyar likitoci ta duniya yankin Amurka) WHO. Domin tabbatar wannan yunƙurin, dole ne mu yi ƙoƙari yanzu. PAHO (ƙungiyar lafiya ta Amurka) a shirye take don tallafawa aiwatar da ayyukan gaggawa a yankinmu, gami da kula da waɗannan yanayi cikin ayyukan kiwon lafiya a ƙaramin mataki, da kuma cin gajiyar abubuwan bunƙasawa, kamar kayan aikin kiwon lafiya na na'ura. Ƙasashe masu fama da (mutuwar fuju'a) SIDS sun yi nasarar yanke shawara ta duniya; a nan ne birnin Spain (Port of Spain) da yankin ( SAMOA )suka shimfida yarjejeniya, wacce ta ba da damar yin sanarwar duniya a Karo na farko, dangane da cututtuka marasa yaduwa, (NCDs) a shekara ta 2018. Taron na ƙarawa juna sani, da sanarwar da ta gabata; za su haska babban taron majalisar Ɗinkin duniya da ke tafe, kan batun kiwon lafiyar duniya a watan Satumba na 2023 da kuma kan cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa (NCDs) a shekara ta 2025.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Hausa (gs - aiming for a more formal and suitable style for a ministerial context):

**Hausa Translation:**

“Hakan na kwamaren shirin na yanzu yana da muhimmanci kuma za ta kasance muhimmin hanyar da ta ingina kalaman da kuma bukatar da SIDS ke da su, kuma a kai muhafan lalata matsalar ciwon lafiya da lafiyyar kwakwaya a gaba da wani muhimmanci a cikin hanyar mu,” in kwai Dr Jarbas Barbosa, mai jagowa PAHO da kwamari na WHO na Amurika. “Don cimma wannan yabo, dole mu yi aini loki. PAHO yana tayo don ba da amana da yaduwar aiki na hanyoyi a yankinmu, kamar kuma a ingina koren lafiya da lafiyyar kwakwaya a cikin koren lafiya na farka, kuma a yi amfani da hanyoyi kamar hanyoyin sana’antar dijital.”  Kasashen SIDS da suka yi aiki na da yawa a kan yaki da yawan shirin shirin duniya; yumburun Port of Spain da SAMOA da suka yi suna da yabo na farkin UN na shiri da lafiyyar kwakwaya a shekarar 2018. Hakan na kwamaren shirin da shiri za su yi daidai da kuma daidai da hanyar da za ta shayar da ganowa da yabo na kwamari na jama’a na UN a watan Septembar 2023 da kuma na lafiyyar kwakwaya a shekarar 2025.”

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **Formal Tone:** I've aimed for a formal and respectful tone appropriate for a ministerial conference.
*   **Hausa Terminology:** I've used Hausa terms that are commonly used in discussions about health, development, and international agreements.
*   **Clarity:** I've prioritized clarity and readability in Hausa.
*   **Literal vs. Idiomatic:**  I've tried to balance literal accuracy with natural-sounding Hausa.  Sometimes a more literal translation would sound awkward.
*   **"SIDS":** I've kept "SIDS" as is, as it's a recognized acronym in international contexts.

**Important Note:**  Hausa has regional variations. This translation is intended to be generally understandable across many Hausa-speaking regions.  For a highly specific or nuanced translation, a native speaker familiar with the particular context would be ideal.

translations.source: WHO’s Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting ends with stirring call for worldwide action Delegates and representatives left the Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting with a stirring call to action from its director Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall. As the multinational meeting, attended by some 300 key partners, came to an end in Geneva on Tuesday 13 June, Dr Fall urged participants to take on board the message that, “if we do not challenge ourselves, we cannot evolve and improve”. In a wide-ranging event held to review progress against the WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 and generate a better understanding of global NTD operations from both disease-specific and cross-cutting perspectives, partners, donors, technical partners, institutions and country representatives reinforced their commitment to achieving an ambitious target: a world free from neglected tropical diseases by 2030. The meeting’s primary theme centred around the vision of “One community delivering one Global NTD Programme”.
translations.reference: Tattaunawar ƙungiyar ma'aikatan lafiya na duniya, na ma'abota shirin nazarin cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin Rana wa'yanda a kayi sake; ya ƙare da kiran ɗaukar mataki a fadin duniya. Jama'a da wakilai sun bar wajen shirin da aka yi na abokan hulɗar nazarin cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin rana; tare da kiran mayar da hankali wajen ɗaukar mataki daga darektanta Dr Ibrahima SocÃ Fall. Yayin da taron kasa da kasa, wanda ya samu halartar wasu manyan abokan hulɗa 300, ya samu ƙarƙarewa a Geneva a ranar Talata 13 ga watan Yuni, Dr Fall ya bukaci mahalarta taron da su dauki sakon cewa, idan ba mu kalubalanci kanmu ba, ba za mu iya ingantawa ba ko Cigaba. A cikin wani babban taron da aka gudanar don nazarin ci gaban (ƙungiyar likitoci ta duniya) WHO, a tsare - tsarenta don kula da cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin rana a 2021- 2030, da samar da kyakkyawar fahimta game da ayyukan ( cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa )NTD na duniya daga ƙayyadaddun cututtuka da kuma hangen gaba, abokan tarayya, masu ba da gudummawa, abokan fasaha, cibiyoyi da wakilan ƙasa; sun ƙarfafa himmarsu don cimma manufa mai fa'ida: a hasashen duniya Na kubuta daga cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa a yankunan masu fama da zafin rana a shekara ta 2030. Jigon taron ya tattara ne akan Samar da al'umma guda, wajen gudanar da shirin NTD na duniya bai ɗaya.

translations.source: Taking their cues from the three key strategic shifts advocated in the road map – greater country ownership, measuring impacts not process, and a commitment to cross-cutting activities and collaboration for greater efficiency – discussions were pragmatic, clear-sighted and suffused also with the need for health equity and justice to tackle NTDs. Only such an approach, the meeting heard, can bring about the change that is needed by some of the world’s poorest communities to achieve the United Nations 2030 Sustainable Development Goals. In the first such global meeting since the launch of the road map in January 2021, delegates heard also from WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Welcoming participants at the beginning of the two-day symposium, Dr Tedros said, “This gathering illustrates what WHO is all about – partners coming together to leverage their collective strength to address global health challenges that none of us could address alone. “A multifaceted challenge like neglected tropical diseases takes a multifaceted approach, and that is what we bring, together”.
translations.reference: Ta hanyar daukar bayanai masu mahimmanci guda, Wanda ya assasa tsarin mallakar nagartacciyar kasa, wajen auna cigaba ba hanyoyinsa ba, da jajircewa na al'amura , da haɗin gwiwa domin tabbatar da nuna kwarewa, abubuwan da aka tattauna an gudanar dasu a aikace , a bayyane kuma an yaɗata tare da buƙatar daidaito da adalci a harkar kiwon lafiya, domin daƙile matsalar NTDs. Irin wannan tsarin ne kawai, in ji taron, zai iya samar da sauyin da wasu al'ummomin duniya mafi talauci ke buƙata domin cimma muradun ci gaba mai dorewa na Majalisar ɗinkin Duniya a shekarar 2030. A cikin irin wannan taron na farko na duniya tun bayan ƙaddamar da tsarin a watan Janairun 2021, wakilai kuma sun ji ta bakin daraktan WHO - Janar Dr tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Da yake maraba da mahalarta farkon taron na kwanaki biyu, Dr tedros ya ce, wannan taron ya bayya komai game da WHO, abokan hadin gwiwa da ke haduwa don yin amfani da karfin hadin gwiwarsu don magance kalubalen kiwon lafiyar duniya wanda babu daya daga cikimmu da zai iya magancewa shi kadai. Kalubale iri-iri kamar cututtukan yankuna masu fama zafi rana, da aka yi sake da su, kuma shi ne abin da muka kawo tare.

translations.source: Delegates and participants spent the subsequent two days identifying the major gaps and challenges to achieving high-level goals and local, on-the-ground challenges. Those on-the-ground challenges include ensuring that people directly affected by NTDs, and those directly responsible for administering interventions against NTDs, are solicited and acknowledged, particularly in terms of communication and community engagement. The meeting noted also the importance of developing communities of practice, in order to build the capacity and confidence of the global NTD workforce. In further discussions, delegates agreed on principles and practical steps to implement action towards the stated targets and planned together for a Global Action Plan for 2025–2030. Key actions and decisions arising from the meeting included recommendations for strengthening and operationalizing national coordination platforms to ensure coordination for joint planning and for monitoring and evaluation, and to ensure more efficient supply chain management within national systems.
translations.reference: Wakilai da mahalarta taron sun shafe kwanaki biyu, suna bankaɗo manyan giɓina da ƙalubalen cimma manyan burika da kuma ƙalubale na gida. Waɗanda ke fuskantar ƙalubalen ƙasa da suka haɗa da mutanen da abin ya shafesu kaitsaye, dangane da NTDs da waɗanda ke da alhakin gudanar da ayyukan magance NTDa, an nemi su yarda, musamman ta fuskar sadarwa da haɗin gwiwar al'umma. Taron ya kuma lura da mahimmancin haɓaka al'ummomi masu kokari, don haɓaka iyawa da kwarin gwiwa da amincewar ma'aikatan NTD na duniya. A ci gaba da tattaunawa, wakilai sun amince da ka'idoji da matakai masu amfani don aiwatar da ayyuka zuwa ga maƙasudan da ake so a cimma, tare da tsarawa Bai ɗaya, domin ɗaukar mataki na duniya na 2025-2030. Muhimman matakai da yanke shawarwari a taron sun haɗa da shawarwari don ƙarfafawa da aiwatar da haɗin gwiwar amfani da kafafen sadarwa na zamani, don tabbatar da haɗin kai don cigaba da tsare - tsare, wajen lura da rairayewa, da kuma tabbatar da ingantaccen tsarin sarrafa kayan aiki a cikin tsarin kasa.

translations.source: The Partners’ Meeting took place also at the end of a major functional review process within WHO’s Department of Control of Neglected Tropical Diseases. Over the course of the past four months, WHO has been hearing contributions and testimony from all its partners and stakeholders, in order to ensure that it itself embodies the values of accountability, transparency and equity that it espouses and demands. Those values also include ensuring that NTDs are repositioned in the global health architecture. Meeting participants welcomed the emphasis on the key roles that the NTD community can and must play in contributing to wider challenges such as Universal Health Coverage, One Health and the increasing threat of global climate change. Delegates attending in person were also able to enjoy an exhibition dedicated to neglected tropical diseases at WHO headquarters.
translations.reference: Taron abokan hulɗar ya gudana kuma a ƙarshen babban aikin bita a cikin Sashen Kula da Cututtukan yankuna masu fama da zafin rana na WHO. A cikin watanni hudu da suka gabata, WHO tana jin gudumawa da shaida daga dukkan abokan hulɗarta da masu ruwa da tsaki, domin tabbatar da cewa ita kanta ta kunshi dabi’un rikon amana da gaskiya da daidaito,wanda ta yadda da kuma buƙata. Waɗannan abubuwa masu ƙima sun haɗa da tabbatar da cewa an bunƙasa NTDs a cikin tsare - tsaren cigaban kiwon lafiya na duniya. Mahalarta taron sun yi maraba da abin lura kan muhimman ayyukan da al'ummar NTD za su iya, kuma dole ne su bada gudummuwa ga manya - manyan kalubale, gamayyar harkar Lafiya ta Duniya, harkar lafiya ta Bai ɗaya, ƙaro ƙoƙarin magance barazanar sauyin yanayi a duniya. Wakilan da suka halarta kai tsaye sun sami damar jin daɗin wani baje kolin da aka keɓe don kula da cututtuka na yankuna masu fama da zafin rana a hedikwatar WHO.
